ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ حَلْحَلَةَ الدُّؤَلِيُّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏ابْنَ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَلِيَّ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُمْ حِينَ قَدِمُوا ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏مِنْ عِنْدِ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏مَقْتَلَ ‏ ‏حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏لَقِيَهُ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرُ بْنُ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ هَلْ لَكَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ حَاجَةٍ تَأْمُرُنِي بِهَا قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ لَا قَالَ لَهُ هَلْ أَنْتَ مُعْطِيَّ سَيْفَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَإِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَغْلِبَكَ الْقَوْمُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَيْمُ اللَّهِ لَئِنْ أَعْطَيْتَنِيهِ لَا يُخْلَصُ إِلَيْهِ أَبَدًا حَتَّى تَبْلُغَ نَفْسِي إِنَّ ‏ ‏عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ‏ ‏خَطَبَ ‏ ‏ابْنَةَ أَبِي جَهْلٍ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏فَاطِمَةَ ‏ ‏فَسَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَخْطُبُ النَّاسَ فِي ذَلِكَ عَلَى مِنْبَرِهِ هَذَا وَأَنَا يَوْمَئِذٍ ‏ ‏مُحْتَلِمٌ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ ‏ ‏فَاطِمَةَ ‏ ‏بَضْعَةٌ مِنِّي وَأَنَا أَتَخَوَّفُ أَنْ ‏ ‏تُفْتَنَ ‏ ‏فِي دِينِهَا قَالَ ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ ‏ ‏صِهْرًا ‏ ‏لَهُ مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي عَبْدِ شَمْسٍ ‏ ‏فَأَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ فِي ‏ ‏مُصَاهَرَتِهِ ‏ ‏إِيَّاهُ فَأَحْسَنَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي فَصَدَقَنِي وَوَعَدَنِي فَوَفَى لِي وَإِنِّي لَسْتُ أُحَرِّمُ حَلَالًا وَلَا أُحِلُّ حَرَامًا وَلَكِنْ وَاللَّهِ ‏ ‏لَا تَجْتَمِعُ ابْنَةُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَابْنَةُ عَدُوِّ اللَّهِ مَكَانًا وَاحِدًا أَبَدًا ‏
en: Yaʿqūb — meaning Ibn Ibrāhīm — narrated to us, my father narrated to us, from al-Walīd ibn Kathīr, Muḥammad ibn ʿAmr ibn Ḥalḥalah al-Duʾalī narrated to me that Ibn Shihāb narrated to him that ʿAlī ibn al-Ḥusayn narrated to him: When they arrived in Madīnah from [the court of] Yazīd ibn Muʿāwiyah after the killing of Ḥusayn ibn ʿAlī, al-Miswar ibn Makhramah met him and said: 'Do you have any need of me that I can fulfill for you?' I said: 'No.' He said: 'Will you give me the sword of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ? For I fear the people may seize it from you. By Allah, if you give it to me, no one shall ever reach it as long as my soul remains in my body. ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib proposed to the daughter of Abū Jahl while married to Fāṭimah, and I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressing the people about that on this pulpit of his — and I was at that time of the age of puberty. He said: Fāṭimah is a part of me, and I fear she will be tried in her religion.' He then mentioned a son-in-law of his from Banū ʿAbd Shams and praised him for his marriage tie with him, saying: 'He spoke to me and was truthful, and he promised me and fulfilled. I do not forbid the lawful nor permit the forbidden, but by Allah, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and the daughter of the enemy of Allah shall never be in the same place.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَخِي ابْنِ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَزَعَمَ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانَ ‏ ‏وَالْمِسْوَرَ بْنَ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَاهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَامَ حِينَ جَاءَهُ ‏ ‏وَفْدُ هَوَازِنَ ‏ ‏مُسْلِمِينَ فَسَأَلُوا أَنْ يَرُدَّ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَالَهُمْ ‏ ‏وَسَبْيَهُمْ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَعِي مَنْ تَرَوْنَ وَأَحَبُّ الْحَدِيثِ إِلَيَّ أَصْدَقُهُ فَاخْتَارُوا إِحْدَى الطَّائِفَتَيْنِ إِمَّا ‏ ‏السَّبْيُ ‏ ‏وَإِمَّا الْمَالُ وَقَدْ كُنْتُ ‏ ‏اسْتَأْنَيْتُ ‏ ‏بِكُمْ وَكَانَ أَنْظَرَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِضْعَ عَشْرَةَ لَيْلَةً حِينَ قَفَلَ مِنْ ‏ ‏الطَّائِفِ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏غَيْرُ رَادٍّ إِلَيْهِمْ إِلَّا إِحْدَى ‏ ‏الطَّائِفَتَيْنِ قَالُوا فَإِنَّا نَخْتَارُ ‏ ‏سَبْيَنَا ‏ ‏فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَأَثْنَى عَلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ بِمَا هُوَ أَهْلُهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَمَّا بَعْدُ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ إِخْوَانَكُمْ قَدْ جَاءُوا تَائِبِينَ وَإِنِّي قَدْ رَأَيْتُ أَنْ أَرُدَّ إِلَيْهِمْ ‏ ‏سَبْيَهُمْ ‏ ‏فَمَنْ أَحَبَّ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ ‏ ‏يُطَيِّبَ ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ فَلْيَفْعَلْ وَمَنْ أَحَبَّ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَكُونَ عَلَى حَظِّهِ حَتَّى نُعْطِيَهُ إِيَّاهُ مِنْ أَوَّلِ مَا ‏ ‏يُفِيءُ ‏ ‏اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَلَيْنَا فَلْيَفْعَلْ فَقَالَ النَّاسُ قَدْ ‏ ‏طَيَّبْنَا ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا لَا نَدْرِي مَنْ أَذِنَ مِنْكُمْ فِي ذَلِكَ مِمَّنْ لَمْ يَأْذَنْ فَارْجِعُوا حَتَّى يَرْفَعَ إِلَيْنَا ‏ ‏عُرَفَاؤُكُمْ ‏ ‏أَمْرَكُمْ فَجَمَعَ النَّاسُ فَكَلَّمَهُمْ ‏ ‏عُرَفَاؤُهُمْ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ رَجَعُوا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَخْبَرُوهُ أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ ‏ ‏طَيَّبُوا ‏ ‏وَأَذِنُوا هَذَا الَّذِي بَلَغَنِي عَنْ ‏ ‏سَبْيِ ‏ ‏هَوَازِنَ ‏
en: Yaʿqūb narrated to us, the nephew of Ibn Shihāb narrated to us, from his uncle who said: ʿUrwah ibn al-Zubayr claimed that Marwān and al-Miswar ibn Makhramah informed him that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood when the delegation of Hawāzin came to him as Muslims, asking him to return their property and captives. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to them: 'With me are those whom you see, and the most beloved speech to me is the most truthful. Choose one of the two: either the captives or the wealth. I had been waiting for you.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ had waited for them over ten nights after returning from al-Ṭāʾif. When it became clear to them that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would not return to them both, they said: 'We choose our captives.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood among the Muslims, praised Allah عز وجل as He deserves, then said: 'As for what follows: your brothers have come repentant, and I have decided to return their captives to them. Whoever among you is willing to let that go freely, let him do so. And whoever among you wishes to hold on to his share until we give it to him from the first thing Allah عز وجل grants us as spoils, let him do so.' The people said: 'We have agreed to that for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'We do not know who among you has consented and who has not. Go back and let your leaders convey your decision to us.' They gathered and their leaders spoke to them, then returned to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and informed him that they had all consented and given permission. This is what has reached me regarding the captives of Hawāzin.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْيَمَانِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏شُعَيْبٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرَ بْنَ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏أخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَمْرَو ابْنَ عَوْفٍ الْأنْصَارِيَّ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ حَلِيفُ ‏ ‏بَنِي عَامِرِ بْنِ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ قَدْ شَهِدَ ‏ ‏بَدْرًا ‏ ‏مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَ ‏ ‏أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ بْنَ الْجَرَّاحِ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏يَأْتِي ‏ ‏بِجِزْيَتِهَا ‏ ‏وَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَالَحَ أَهْلَ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏وَأَمَّرَ عَلَيْهِمْ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءَ بْنَ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏فَقَدِمَ ‏ ‏أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ ‏ ‏بِمَالٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي مِثْلَ حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعَتْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ ‏ ‏قَدِمَ بِمَالٍ مِنْ قِبَلِ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَهُ عَلَى ‏ ‏الْبَحْرَيْنِ ‏ ‏فَوَافَوْا ‏ ‏مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَاةَ الصُّبْحِ فَلَمَّا انْصَرَفَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَعَرَّضُوا فَلَمَّا رَآهُمْ تَبَسَّمَ وَقَالَ لَعَلَّكُمْ سَمِعْتُمْ أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا عُبَيْدَةَ بْنَ الْجَرَّاحِ ‏ ‏قَدِمَ وَقَدِمَ بِمَالٍ قَالُوا أَجَلْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبْشِرُوا وَأَمِّلُوا خَيْرًا فَوَاللَّهِ ‏ ‏مَا الْفَقْرُ أَخْشَى عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَكِنْ إِذَا صُبَّتْ عَلَيْكُمْ الدُّنْيَا فَتَنَافَسْتُمُوهَا كَمَا تَنَافَسَهَا مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ ‏
en: Abū al-Yamān narrated to us, Shuʿayb informed us, from al-Zuhrī, ʿUrwah ibn al-Zubayr narrated to us that al-Miswar ibn Makhramah informed him that ʿAmr ibn ʿAwf al-Anṣārī — who was an ally of Banū ʿĀmir ibn Luʾayy and had been present at Badr with the Prophet ﷺ — [informed him of a long hadith about the Prophet ﷺ appointing governors and handling administrative matters].

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏رَوْحٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرَ بْنَ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏أخْبَرَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏وَثَنَا ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الطَّبَّاعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏مَالِكٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِشَامٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏سُبَيْعَةَ الْأَسْلَمِيَّةَ ‏ ‏نُفِسَتْ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ وَفَاةِ زَوْجِهَا بِلَيَالٍ فَقَالَ لَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏حَلَلْتِ ‏ ‏فَانْكِحِي ‏
en: Rawḥ narrated to us, Mālik ibn Anas narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ʿUrwah, from his father, that al-Miswar ibn Makhramah informed him — and Isḥāq, meaning Ibn al-Ṭabbāʿ, narrated to us, Mālik informed me, from Hishām, from his father, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah — that Subayʿah al-Aslamiyyah gave birth a few nights after her husband died, and she came to the Prophet ﷺ and asked permission to remarry, and he gave her permission. So she married.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ أُسَامَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏سُبَيْعَةَ الْأَسْلَمِيَّةَ ‏ ‏تُوُفِّيَ عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا وَهِيَ حَامِلٌ فَلَمْ تَمْكُثْ إِلَّا ‏ ‏لَيَالِيَ حَتَّى وَضَعَتْ فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏تَعَلَّتْ ‏ ‏مِنْ نِفَاسِهَا خُطِبَتْ فَاسْتَأْذَنَتْ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي النِّكَاحِ ‏ ‏فَأَذِنَ لَهَا أَنْ تَنْكِحَ فَنَكَحَتْ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَرَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَضَعَتْ ‏ ‏سُبَيْعَةُ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Usāmah narrated to us, Hishām informed us, from his father, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah, that Subayʿah al-Aslamiyyah's husband died while she was pregnant. She waited only a few nights before she gave birth. When she recovered from her postpartum period, she was proposed to. She sought the permission of the Prophet ﷺ to marry, and he gave her permission. So she married.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَمَرْوَانَ ‏ ‏قَالَا ‏ ‏قَلَّدَ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيَ ‏ ‏وَأَشْعَرَهُ ‏ ‏بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ ‏ ‏وَأَحْرَمَ مِنْهَا بِالْعُمْرَةِ ‏ ‏حَلَقَ ‏ ‏بِالْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏فِي عُمْرَتِهِ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَ أَصْحَابَهُ بِذَلِكَ وَنَحَرَ ‏ ‏بِالْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ وَأَمَرَ أَصْحَابَهُ بِذَلِكَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar informed us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUrwah ibn al-Zubayr, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwān who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ garlanded the sacrificial animals and marked them at Dhū al-Ḥulayfah, entered iḥrām for ʿumrah from there, and shaved his head.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَوْفِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ابْنُ أَخِي ‏ ‏عَائِشَةَ ‏ ‏لِأُمِّهَا ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَتْهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فِي بَيْعٍ أَوْ عَطَاءٍ أَعْطَتْهُ وَاللَّهِ لَتَنْتَهِيَنَّ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةُ ‏ ‏أَوَ لَأَحْجُرَنَّ عَلَيْهَا فَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةُ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا ‏ ‏أَوَقَالَ هَذَا قَالُوا نَعَمْ قَالَتْ هُوَ لِلَّهِ عَلَيَّ نَذْرٌ أَنْ لَا أُكَلِّمَ ‏ ‏ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏كَلِمَةً أَبَدًا فَاسْتَشْفَعَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرَ بْنَ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ يَغُوثَ ‏ ‏وَهُمَا مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي زُهْرَةَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏ ‏وَطَفِقَ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرُ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏يُنَاشِدَانِ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةَ ‏ ‏إِلَّا كَلَّمَتْهُ وَقَبِلَتْ مِنْهُ وَيَقُولَانِ لَهَا إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ نَهَى عَمَّا قَدْ عَلِمْتِ مِنْ الْهَجْرِ إِنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَا يَحِلُّ لِمُسْلِمٍ أَنْ يَهْجُرَ أَخَاهُ فَوْقَ ثَلَاثِ لَيَالٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَوْزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الطُّفَيْلِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ رَجُلًا مِنْ ‏ ‏أَزْدِ شَنُوءَةَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ أَخًا ‏ ‏لِعَائِشَةَ ‏ ‏لِأُمِّهَا ‏ ‏أُمِّ رُومَانَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ فَاسْتَعَانَ عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏بِالْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ يَغُوثَ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَأْذَنَا عَلَيْهَا فَأَذِنَتْ لَهُمَا فَكَلَّمَاهَا وَنَاشَدَاهَا اللَّهَ وَالْقَرَابَةَ وَقَوْلَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَحِلُّ لِامْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَنْ يَهْجُرَ أَخَاهُ فَوْقَ ثَلَاثٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْيَمَانِ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏شُعَيْبٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَوْفُ بْنُ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ الطُّفَيْلِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ابْنُ أَخِي ‏ ‏عَائِشَةَ زَوْجِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِأُمِّهَا أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَائِشَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَتْهُ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿAwf ibn al-Ḥārith — who was the son of ʿĀʾishah's sister from her mother — that ʿĀʾishah narrated to him that ʿAbd Allāh ibn al-Zubayr said regarding a sale or a gift she had made: 'By Allah, ʿĀʾishah must stop, or I shall place her under guardianship.' [This hadith relates to a dispute about ʿĀʾishah's spending.]

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانَ ‏ ‏وَالْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ أَحَدُهُمَا عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَامَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏فِي بِضْعَ عَشْرَةَ مِائَةً مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ فَلَمَّا كَانَ ‏ ‏بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ ‏ ‏قَلَّدَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيَ ‏ ‏وَأَشْعَرَ ‏ ‏وَأَحْرَمَ مِنْهَا ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏مَرَّةً مِنْ عُمْرَةٍ وَلَمْ يُسَمِّ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرَ ‏ ‏وَبَعَثَ عَيْنًا لَهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَسَارَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا ‏
en: Sufyān ibn ʿUyaynah narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUrwah ibn al-Zubayr, from Marwān and al-Miswar ibn Makhramah — each adding to what the other related: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ set out in the year of al-Ḥudaybiyah with over one thousand of his Companions. When he reached [Dhū al-Ḥulayfah, the full Ḥudaybiyah narration follows as in the prior chains].

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏لَيْثٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِرَاكٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏سَمِعَ ‏ ‏مَرْوَانَ ‏ ‏بِالْمَوْسِمِ يَقُولُ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَطَعَ فِي ‏ ‏مِجَنٍّ ‏ ‏وَالْبَعِيرُ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْمِجَنِّ ‏
en: Yūnus ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, Layth — meaning Ibn Saʿd — narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī Ḥabīb, from ʿIrāk, that he heard Marwān during the Ḥajj season saying: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ [imposed the punishment of] cutting [the hand] for [stealing] a shield, and a camel is worth more than a shield.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏اللَّيْثُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ يَقُولُ إِنَّ ‏ ‏بَنِي هِشَامِ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ ‏ ‏اسْتَأْذَنُونِي فِي أَنْ يُنْكِحُوا ‏ ‏ابْنَتَهُمْ ‏ ‏عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ‏ ‏فَلَا آذَنُ لَهُمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا آذَنُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَا آذَنُ فَإِنَّمَا ابْنَتِي ‏ ‏بَضْعَةٌ ‏ ‏مِنِّي ‏ ‏يُرِيبُنِي ‏ ‏مَا ‏ ‏أَرَابَهَا ‏ ‏وَيُؤْذِينِي مَا آذَاهَا ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, al-Layth ibn Saʿd narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿUbayd Allāh ibn Abī Mulaykah narrated to me, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was on the pulpit saying: "Indeed, Banū Hishām ibn al-Mughīrah have asked my permission to marry their daughter to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib. I do not give them permission." Then he said: "I do not give them permission." Then he said: "I do not give them permission. For indeed my daughter is but a part of me; what disturbs her disturbs me, and what harms her harms me."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏لَيْثٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أُهْدِيَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَقْبِيَةٌ ‏ ‏مُزَرَّرَةٌ ‏ ‏بِالذَّهَبِ ‏ ‏فَقَسَمَهَا فِي أَصْحَابِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَخْرَمَةُ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏مِسْوَرُ ‏ ‏اذْهَبْ بِنَا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ ذُكِرَ لِي أَنَّهُ قَسَمَ ‏ ‏أَقْبِيَةً ‏ ‏فَانْطَلَقْنَا فَقَالَ ادْخُلْ فَادْعُهُ لِي قَالَ فَدَخَلْتُ فَدَعَوْتُهُ إِلَيْهِ فَخَرَجَ إِلَيَّ وَعَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏قَبَاءٌ ‏ ‏مِنْهَا قَالَ خَبَأْتُ لَكَ هَذَا يَا ‏ ‏مَخْرَمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَنَظَرَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ رَضِيَ فَأَعْطَاهُ إِيَّاهُ ‏
en: Hāshim narrated to us, Layth narrated to us, ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿUbayd Allāh ibn Abī Mulaykah narrated to me, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah, who said: Cloaks fastened with gold buttons were gifted to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he distributed them among his Companions. Makhramah said: "O Miswar, come take us to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, for I have been told that he distributed some cloaks." So we went, and he said: "Enter and call him out for me." I entered and called him, and he came out to me wearing one of the cloaks. He said: "I saved this for you, O Makhramah." Makhramah looked at it and said: "Raḍiya (I am satisfied)." And he gave it to him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَمَرْوَانَ بَنِ الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏يُصَدِّقُ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا حَدِيثَ صَاحِبِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَا ‏ ‏خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏زَمَانَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏فِي بِضْعَ عَشْرَةَ مِائَةً مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانُوا ‏ ‏بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ ‏ ‏قَلَّدَ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْهَدْيَ ‏ ‏وَأَشْعَرَهُ ‏ ‏وَأَحْرَمَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ وَبَعَثَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ‏ ‏عَيْنًا ‏ ‏لَهُ مِنْ ‏ ‏خُزَاعَةَ ‏ ‏يُخْبِرُهُ عَنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏وَسَارَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ ‏ ‏بِغَدِيرِ الْأَشْطَاطِ ‏ ‏قَرِيبٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏عُسْفَانَ ‏ ‏أَتَاهُ ‏ ‏عَيْنُهُ ‏ ‏الْخُزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنِّي قَدْ تَرَكْتُ ‏ ‏كَعْبَ بْنَ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏وَعَامِرَ بْنَ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكَ ‏ ‏الْأَحَابِشَ ‏ ‏وَجَمَعُوا لَكَ جُمُوعًا وَهُمْ مُقَاتِلُوكَ وَصَادُّوكَ عَنْ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَشِيرُوا عَلَيَّ أَتَرَوْنَ أَنْ نَمِيلَ إِلَى ‏ ‏ذَرَارِيِّ ‏ ‏هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَعَانُوهُمْ فَنُصِيبَهُمْ فَإِنْ قَعَدُوا قَعَدُوا ‏ ‏مَوْتُورِينَ ‏ ‏مَحْرُوبِينَ ‏ ‏وَإِنْ نَجَوْا ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏مَحْزُونِينَ وَإِنْ يَحْنُونَ ‏ ‏تَكُنْ عُنُقًا قَطَعَهَا اللَّهُ أَوْ تَرَوْنَ أَنْ ‏ ‏نَؤُمَّ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتَ ‏ ‏فَمَنْ صَدَّنَا عَنْهُ قَاتَلْنَاهُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ إِنَّمَا جِئْنَا مُعْتَمِرِينَ وَلَمْ نَجِئْ نُقَاتِلُ أَحَدًا وَلَكِنْ مَنْ حَالَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏قَاتَلْنَاهُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَرُوحُوا إِذًا قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ ‏ ‏أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ مَا رَأَيْتُ أَحَدًا قَطُّ كَانَ أَكْثَرَ مَشُورَةً لِأَصْحَابِهِ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَمَرْوَانَ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏فَرَاحُوا حَتَّى إِذَا كَانُوا بِبَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ ‏ ‏خَالِدَ بْنَ الْوَلِيدِ ‏ ‏بِالْغَمِيمِ ‏ ‏فِي خَيْلٍ ‏ ‏لِقُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏طَلِيعَةً ‏ ‏فَخُذُوا ذَاتَ الْيَمِينِ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا شَعَرَ ‏ ‏بِهِمْ ‏ ‏خَالِدٌ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا هُوَ ‏ ‏بِقَتَرَةِ ‏ ‏الْجَيْشِ فَانْطَلَقَ ‏ ‏يَرْكُضُ ‏ ‏نَذِيرًا ‏ ‏لِقُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏وَسَارَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ ‏ ‏بِالثَّنِيَّةِ ‏ ‏الَّتِي يَهْبِطُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْهَا بَرَكَتْ بِهِ ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتُهُ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏بَرَكَتْ بِهَا ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتُهُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَلْ ‏ ‏حَلْ ‏ ‏فَأَلَحَّتْ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا ‏ ‏خَلَأَتْ ‏ ‏الْقَصْوَاءُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا ‏ ‏خَلَأَتْ ‏ ‏الْقَصْوَاءُ ‏ ‏وَمَا ذَاكَ لَهَا بِخُلُقٍ وَلَكِنْ ‏ ‏حَبَسَهَا ‏ ‏حَابِسُ ‏ ‏الْفِيلِ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ ‏ ‏لَا يَسْأَلُونِي خُطَّةً يُعَظِّمُونَ فِيهَا حُرُمَاتِ اللَّهِ إِلَّا أَعْطَيْتُهُمْ إِيَّاهَا ثُمَّ ‏ ‏زَجَرَهَا ‏ ‏فَوَثَبَتْ بِهِ قَالَ فَعَدَلَ عَنْهَا حَتَّى نَزَلَ بِأَقْصَى ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏ثَمَدٍ ‏ ‏قَلِيلِ الْمَاءِ إِنَّمَا ‏ ‏يَتَبَرَّضُهُ ‏ ‏النَّاسُ ‏ ‏تَبَرُّضًا ‏ ‏فَلَمْ يَلْبَثْهُ النَّاسُ أَنْ ‏ ‏نَزَحُوهُ ‏ ‏فَشُكِيَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْعَطَشُ فَانْتَزَعَ سَهْمًا مِنْ ‏ ‏كِنَانَتِهِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُمْ أَنْ يَجْعَلُوهُ فِيهِ قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا زَالَ ‏ ‏يَجِيشُ ‏ ‏لَهُمْ بِالرِّيِّ حَتَّى صَدَرُوا عَنْهُ قَالَ فَبَيْنَمَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ جَاءَ ‏ ‏بُدَيْلُ بْنُ وَرْقَاءَ الْخُزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏نَفَرٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ قَوْمِهِ وَكَانُوا ‏ ‏عَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏نُصْحٍ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏تِهَامَةَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ إِنِّي تَرَكْتُ ‏ ‏كَعْبَ بْنَ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏وَعَامِرَ بْنَ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏نَزَلُوا أَعْدَادَ مِيَاهِ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏مَعَهُمْ ‏ ‏الْعُوذُ ‏ ‏الْمَطَافِيلُ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ مُقَاتِلُوكَ وَصَادُّوكَ عَنْ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا لَمْ نَجِئْ لِقِتَالِ أَحَدٍ وَلَكِنَّا جِئْنَا مُعْتَمِرِينَ وَإِنَّ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشًا ‏ ‏قَدْ نَهَكَتْهُمْ الْحَرْبُ ‏ ‏فَأَضَرَّتْ ‏ ‏بِهِمْ فَإِنْ شَاءُوا مَادَدْتُهُمْ ‏ ‏مُدَّةً ‏ ‏وَيُخَلُّوا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ النَّاسِ فَإِنْ أَظْهَرُ فَإِنْ شَاءُوا أَنْ يَدْخُلُوا فِيمَا دَخَلَ فِيهِ النَّاسُ فَعَلُوا وَإِلَّا فَقَدْ ‏ ‏جَمَوْا ‏ ‏وَإِنْ هُمْ أَبَوْا وَإِلَّا فَوَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ لَأُقَاتِلَنَّهُمْ عَلَى أَمْرِي هَذَا حَتَّى تَنْفَرِدَ ‏ ‏سَالِفَتِي ‏ ‏أَوْ لَيُنْفِذَنَّ اللَّهُ أَمْرَهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏حَتَّى تَنْفَرِدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَإِنْ شَاءُوا مَادَدْنَاهُمْ ‏ ‏مُدَّةً ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بُدَيْلٌ ‏ ‏سَأُبَلِّغُهُمْ مَا تَقُولُ فَانْطَلَقَ حَتَّى أَتَى ‏ ‏قُرَيْشًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّا قَدْ جِئْنَاكُمْ مِنْ عِنْدِ هَذَا الرَّجُلِ وَسَمِعْنَاهُ يَقُولُ قَوْلًا فَإِنْ شِئْتُمْ نَعْرِضُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَقَالَ سُفَهَاؤُهُمْ لَا حَاجَةَ لَنَا فِي أَنْ تُحَدِّثَنَا عَنْهُ بِشَيْءٍ وَقَالَ ذُو الرَّأْيِ مِنْهُمْ هَاتِ مَا سَمِعْتَهُ يَقُولُ قَالَ قَدْ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ كَذَا وَكَذَا فَحَدَّثَهُمْ بِمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ بْنُ مَسْعُودٍ الثَّقَفِيُّ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ أَيْ قَوْمُ أَلَسْتُمْ بِالْوَالِدِ قَالُوا بَلَى قَالَ أَوَلَسْتُ بِالْوَلَدِ قَالُوا بَلَى قَالَ فَهَلْ تَتَّهِمُونِي قَالُوا لَا قَالَ أَلَسْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ أَنِّي اسْتَنْفَرْتُ أَهْلَ ‏ ‏عُكَاظٍ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏بَلَّحُوا ‏ ‏عَلَيَّ جِئْتُكُمْ بِأَهْلِي وَمَنْ أَطَاعَنِي قَالُوا بَلَى فَقَالَ إِنَّ هَذَا قَدْ عَرَضَ عَلَيْكُمْ خُطَّةَ ‏ ‏رُشْدٍ ‏ ‏فَاقْبَلُوهَا وَدَعُونِي آتِهِ فَقَالُوا ائْتِهِ فَأَتَاهُ قَالَ فَجَعَلَ يُكَلِّمُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ نَحْوًا مِنْ قَوْلِهِ ‏ ‏لِبُدَيْلٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ ‏ ‏عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ أَيْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ ‏ ‏أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ اسْتَأْصَلْتَ قَوْمَكَ هَلْ سَمِعْتَ بِأَحَدٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏اجْتَاحَ أَهْلَهُ قَبْلَكَ وَإِنْ تَكُنْ الْأُخْرَى فَوَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأَرَى وُجُوهًا وَأَرَى ‏ ‏أَوْبَاشًا ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّاسِ خُلُقًا أَنْ يَفِرُّوا وَيَدَعُوكَ فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ ‏ ‏امْصُصْ ‏ ‏بَظْرَ اللَّاتِ نَحْنُ نَفِرُّ عَنْهُ وَنَدَعُهُ فَقَالَ مَنْ ذَا قَالُوا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَمَا وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ لَوْلَا يَدٌ كَانَتْ لَكَ عِنْدِي لَمْ أَجْزِكَ بِهَا لَأَجَبْتُكَ وَجَعَلَ يُكَلِّمُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَكُلَّمَا كَلَّمَهُ أَخَذَ بِلِحْيَتِهِ ‏ ‏وَالْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ شُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَائِمٌ عَلَى رَأْسِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَمَعَهُ السَّيْفُ وَعَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏الْمِغْفَرُ ‏ ‏وَكُلَّمَا أَهْوَى ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ ‏ ‏بِيَدِهِ إِلَى لِحْيَةِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ضَرَبَ يَدَهُ ‏ ‏بِنَصْلِ ‏ ‏السَّيْفِ وَقَالَ أَخِّرْ يَدَكَ عَنْ لِحْيَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَرَفَعَ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةُ ‏ ‏يَدَهُ فَقَالَ مَنْ هَذَا قَالُوا ‏ ‏الْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ شُعْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَيْ ‏ ‏غُدَرُ أَوَلَسْتُ أَسْعَى فِي ‏ ‏غَدْرَتِكَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ ‏ ‏الْمُغِيرَةُ ‏ ‏صَحِبَ قَوْمًا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَتَلَهُمْ وَأَخَذَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ ثُمَّ جَاءَ فَأَسْلَمَ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَّا الْإِسْلَامُ فَأَقْبَلُ وَأَمَّا الْمَالُ فَلَسْتُ مِنْهُ فِي شَيْءٍ ثُمَّ إِنَّ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏جَعَلَ ‏ ‏يَرْمُقُ ‏ ‏النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِعَيْنِهِ قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا ‏ ‏تَنَخَّمَ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نُخَامَةً ‏ ‏إِلَّا وَقَعَتْ فِي كَفِّ رَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ فَدَلَكَ بِهَا وَجْهَهُ وَجِلْدَهُ وَإِذَا أَمَرَهُمْ ‏ ‏ابْتَدَرُوا ‏ ‏أَمْرَهُ وَإِذَا تَوَضَّأَ كَادُوا يَقْتَتِلُونَ عَلَى وَضُوئِهِ وَإِذَا تَكَلَّمُوا خَفَضُوا أَصْوَاتَهُمْ عِنْدَهُ وَمَا يُحِدُّونَ إِلَيْهِ النَّظَرَ تَعْظِيمًا لَهُ فَرَجَعَ إِلَى أَصْحَابِهِ فَقَالَ أَيْ قَوْمِ وَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ وَفَدْتُ عَلَى الْمُلُوكِ وَوَفَدْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏قَيْصَرَ ‏ ‏وَكِسْرَى ‏ ‏وَالنَّجَاشِيِّ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ إِنْ رَأَيْتُ مَلِكًا قَطُّ يُعَظِّمُهُ أَصْحَابُهُ مَا يُعَظِّمُ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدًا ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ إِنْ ‏ ‏يَتَنَخَّمُ ‏ ‏نُخَامَةً ‏ ‏إِلَّا وَقَعَتْ فِي كَفَّ رَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ فَدَلَكَ بِهَا وَجْهَهُ وَجِلْدَهُ وَإِذَا أَمَرَهُمْ ‏ ‏ابْتَدَرُوا ‏ ‏أَمْرَهُ وَإِذَا تَوَضَّأَ كَادُوا يَقْتَتِلُونَ عَلَى وَضُوئِهِ وَإِذَا تَكَلَّمُوا خَفَضُوا أَصْوَاتَهُمْ عِنْدَهُ وَمَا يُحِدُّونَ إِلَيْهِ النَّظَرَ تَعْظِيمًا لَهُ وَإِنَّهُ قَدْ عَرَضَ عَلَيْكُمْ خُطَّةَ رُشْدٍ فَاقْبَلُوهَا فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي كِنَانَةَ ‏ ‏دَعُونِي آتِهِ فَقَالُوا ائْتِهِ فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏أَشْرَفَ ‏ ‏عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَذَا فُلَانٌ وَهُوَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ يُعَظِّمُونَ ‏ ‏الْبُدْنَ ‏ ‏فَابْعَثُوهَا لَهُ فَبُعِثَتْ لَهُ وَاسْتَقْبَلَهُ الْقَوْمُ يُلَبُّونَ فَلَمَّا رَأَى ذَلِكَ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ مَا يَنْبَغِي لِهَؤُلَاءِ أَنْ يُصَدُّوا عَنْ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ إِلَى أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏الْبُدْنَ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏قُلِّدَتْ ‏ ‏وَأُشْعِرَتْ ‏ ‏فَلَمْ أَرَ أَنْ يُصَدُّوا عَنْ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏مِكْرَزُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ دَعُونِي آتِهِ فَقَالُوا ائْتِهِ فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏أَشْرَفَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِمْ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَذَا ‏ ‏مِكْرَزٌ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ رَجُلٌ فَاجِرٌ فَجَعَلَ يُكَلِّمُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَبَيْنَا هُوَ يُكَلِّمُهُ إِذْ جَاءَهُ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏وَأَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَمَّا جَاءَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَهُلَ مِنْ أَمْرِكُمْ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏فِي حَدِيثِهِ فَجَاءَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏فَقَالَ هَاتِ اكْتُبْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ كِتَابًا فَدَعَا الْكَاتِبَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اكْتُبْ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏أَمَّا الرَّحْمَنُ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا أَدْرِي مَا هُوَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏مَا هُوَ ‏ ‏وَلَكِنْ اكْتُبْ بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ كَمَا كُنْتَ تَكْتُبُ فَقَالَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ وَاللَّهِ مَا نَكْتُبُهَا إِلَّا بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اكْتُبْ بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ ثُمَّ قَالَ هَذَا مَا ‏ ‏قَاضَى ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ لَوْ كُنَّا نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ مَا صَدَدْنَاكَ عَنْ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏وَلَا قَاتَلْنَاكَ وَلَكِنْ اكْتُبْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَإِنْ كَذَّبْتُمُونِي اكْتُبْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏وَذَلِكَ لِقَوْلِهِ لَا يَسْأَلُونِي خُطَّةً يُعَظِّمُونَ فِيهَا حُرُمَاتِ اللَّهِ إِلَّا أَعْطَيْتُهُمْ إِيَّاهَا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى أَنْ تُخَلُّوا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ ‏ ‏الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏فَنَطُوفَ بِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏وَاللَّهِ لَا تَتَحَدَّثُ ‏ ‏الْعَرَبُ ‏ ‏أَنَّا أُخِذْنَا ‏ ‏ضُغْطَةً ‏ ‏وَلَكِنْ لَكَ مِنْ الْعَامِ الْمُقْبِلِ فَكَتَبَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏عَلَى أَنَّهُ لَا يَأْتِيكَ مِنَّا رَجُلٌ وَإِنْ كَانَ عَلَى دِينِكَ إِلَّا رَدَدْتَهُ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُرَدُّ إِلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَقَدْ جَاءَ مُسْلِمًا فَبَيْنَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ جَاءَ ‏ ‏أَبُو جَنْدَلِ بْنُ سُهَيْلِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏يَرْسُفُ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏يَرْصُفُ ‏ ‏فِي قُيُودِهِ وَقَدْ خَرَجَ مِنْ أَسْفَلِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى رَمَى بِنَفْسِهِ بَيْنَ أَظْهُرِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏سُهَيْلٌ ‏ ‏هَذَا يَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ ‏ ‏أَوَّلُ مَا ‏ ‏أُقَاضِيكَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ أَنْ تَرُدَّهُ إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّا لَمْ نَقْضِ الْكِتَابَ بَعْدُ قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ إِذًا لَا نُصَالِحُكَ عَلَى شَيْءٍ أَبَدًا فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَجِزْهُ ‏ ‏لِي قَالَ مَا أَنَا ‏ ‏بِمُجِيزُهُ لَكَ قَالَ بَلَى فَافْعَلْ قَالَ مَا أَنَا بِفَاعِلٍ قَالَ ‏ ‏مِكْرَزٌ ‏ ‏بَلَى قَدْ ‏ ‏أَجَزْنَاهُ ‏ ‏لَكَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو جَنْدَلٍ ‏ ‏أَيْ مَعَاشِرَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ أُرَدُّ إِلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَقَدْ جِئْتُ مُسْلِمًا أَلَا تَرَوْنَ مَا قَدْ لَقِيتُ وَكَانَ قَدْ عُذِّبَ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا فِي اللَّهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَنْهُ ‏ ‏فَأَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ أَلَسْتَ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ قَالَ بَلَى قُلْتُ أَلَسْنَا عَلَى الْحَقِّ وَعَدُوُّنَا عَلَى الْبَاطِلِ قَالَ بَلَى قُلْتُ فَلِمَ ‏ ‏نُعْطِي ‏ ‏الدَّنِيَّةَ ‏ ‏فِي دِينِنَا إِذًا قَالَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَلَسْتُ أَعْصِيهِ وَهُوَ نَاصِرِي قُلْتُ أَوَلَسْتَ كُنْتَ تُحَدِّثُنَا أَنَّا سَنَأْتِي ‏ ‏الْبَيْتَ ‏ ‏فَنَطُوفُ بِهِ قَالَ بَلَى قَالَ أَفَأَخْبَرْتُكَ أَنَّكَ تَأْتِيهِ الْعَامَ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ آتِيهِ وَمُتَطَوِّفٌ بِهِ قَالَ فَأَتَيْتُ ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَنْهُ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏أَلَيْسَ هَذَا نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ حَقًّا قَالَ بَلَى قُلْتُ أَلَسْنَا عَلَى الْحَقِّ وَعَدُوُّنَا عَلَى الْبَاطِلِ قَالَ بَلَى قُلْتُ فَلِمَ ‏ ‏نُعْطِي ‏ ‏الدَّنِيَّةَ ‏ ‏فِي دِينِنَا إِذًا قَالَ أَيُّهَا الرَّجُلُ إِنَّهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَلَيْسَ يَعْصِي رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ وَهُوَ نَاصِرُهُ فَاسْتَمْسِكْ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏بِغَرْزِهِ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ تَطَوَّفْ ‏ ‏بِغَرْزِهِ ‏ ‏حَتَّى تَمُوتَ فَوَاللَّهِ إِنَّهُ لَعَلَى الْحَقِّ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ أَوَلَيْسَ كَانَ يُحَدِّثُنَا أَنَّا سَنَأْتِي ‏ ‏الْبَيْتَ ‏ ‏وَنَطُوفُ بِهِ قَالَ بَلَى قَالَ أَفَأَخْبَرَكَ أَنَّهُ يَأْتِيهِ الْعَامَ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ آتِيهِ وَمُتَطَوِّفٌ بِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏ ‏فَعَمِلْتُ لِذَلِكَ أَعْمَالًا ‏ ‏قَالَ فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ مِنْ قَضِيَّةِ الْكِتَابِ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِأَصْحَابِهِ قُومُوا فَانْحَرُوا ثُمَّ احْلِقُوا قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا قَامَ مِنْهُمْ رَجُلٌ حَتَّى قَالَ ذَلِكَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ فَلَمَّا لَمْ يَقُمْ مِنْهُمْ أَحَدٌ قَامَ فَدَخَلَ عَلَى ‏ ‏أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ لَهَا مَا لَقِيَ مِنْ النَّاسِ فَقَالَتْ ‏ ‏أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَتُحِبُّ ذَلِكَ اخْرُجْ ثُمَّ لَا تُكَلِّمْ أَحَدًا مِنْهُمْ كَلِمَةً حَتَّى تَنْحَرَ ‏ ‏بُدْنَكَ ‏ ‏وَتَدْعُوَ حَالِقَكَ فَيَحْلِقَكَ فَقَامَ فَخَرَجَ فَلَمْ يُكَلِّمْ أَحَدًا مِنْهُمْ حَتَّى فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ نَحَرَ ‏ ‏هَدْيَهُ ‏ ‏وَدَعَا حَالِقَهُ فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا ذَلِكَ قَامُوا فَنَحَرُوا وَجَعَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ يَحْلِقُ بَعْضًا حَتَّى كَادَ بَعْضُهُمْ يَقْتُلُ بَعْضًا غَمًّا ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ نِسْوَةٌ مُؤْمِنَاتٌ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا جَاءَكُمْ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ مُهَاجِرَاتٍ ‏ ‏حَتَّى بَلَغَ ‏ ‏بِعِصَمِ الْكَوَافِرِ ‏} ‏قَالَ فَطَلَّقَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏ ‏يَوْمَئِذٍ امْرَأَتَيْنِ كَانَتَا لَهُ فِي الشِّرْكِ فَتَزَوَّجَ إِحْدَاهُمَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏وَالْأُخْرَى ‏ ‏صَفْوَانُ بْنُ أُمَيَّةَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ مُسْلِمٌ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏فَقَدِمَ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرِ بْنُ أُسَيْدٍ الثَّقَفِيُّ ‏ ‏مُسْلِمًا مُهَاجِرًا ‏ ‏فَاسْتَأْجَرَ ‏ ‏الْأَخْنَسَ بْنَ شَرِيقٍ ‏ ‏رَجُلًا كَافِرًا مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي عَامِرِ بْنِ لُؤَيٍّ ‏ ‏وَمَوْلًى ‏ ‏مَعَهُ وَكَتَبَ مَعَهُمَا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَسْأَلُهُ الْوَفَاءَ فَأَرْسَلُوا فِي طَلَبِهِ رَجُلَيْنِ فَقَالُوا الْعَهْدَ الَّذِي جَعَلْتَ لَنَا فِيهِ فَدَفَعَهُ إِلَى الرَّجُلَيْنِ فَخَرَجَا بِهِ حَتَّى بَلَغَا بِهِ ‏ ‏ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ ‏ ‏فَنَزَلُوا يَأْكُلُونَ مِنْ تَمْرٍ لَهُمْ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏لِأَحَدِ الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأَرَى سَيْفَكَ يَا فُلَانُ هَذَا جَيِّدًا ‏ ‏فَاسْتَلَّهُ ‏ ‏الْآخَرُ فَقَالَ أَجَلْ وَاللَّهِ إِنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَجَيِّدٌ ‏ ‏لَقَدْ ‏ ‏جَرَّبْتُ ‏ ‏بِهِ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏جَرَّبْتُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْهِ فَأَمْكَنَهُ مِنْهُ فَضَرَبَهُ حَتَّى بَرَدَ وَفَرَّ الْآخَرُ حَتَّى أَتَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏فَدَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ ‏ ‏يَعْدُو فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَقَدْ رَأَى هَذَا ‏ ‏ذُعْرًا ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا انْتَهَى إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُتِلَ وَاللَّهِ صَاحِبِي وَإِنِّي لَمَقْتُولٌ فَجَاءَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ قَدْ وَاللَّهِ أَوْفَى اللَّهُ ‏ ‏ذِمَّتَكَ ‏ ‏قَدْ رَدَدْتَنِي إِلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ أَنْجَانِي اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَيْلُ أُمِّهِ ‏ ‏مِسْعَرَ ‏ ‏حَرْبٍ لَوْ كَانَ لَهُ أَحَدٌ فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ ذَلِكَ عَرَفَ أَنَّهُ سَيَرُدُّهُ إِلَيْهِمْ فَخَرَجَ حَتَّى أَتَى ‏ ‏سِيفَ ‏ ‏الْبَحْرِ قَالَ وَيَتَفَلَّتُ ‏ ‏أَبُو جَنْدَلِ بْنُ سُهَيْلٍ ‏ ‏فَلَحِقَ ‏ ‏بِأَبِي بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏فَجَعَلَ لَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ قَدْ أَسْلَمَ إِلَّا لَحِقَ ‏ ‏بِأَبِي بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏حَتَّى اجْتَمَعَتْ مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏عِصَابَةٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا يَسْمَعُونَ بِعِيرٍ خَرَجَتْ ‏ ‏لِقُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏الشَّامِ ‏ ‏إِلَّا اعْتَرَضُوا لَهَا فَقَتَلُوهُمْ وَأَخَذُوا أَمْوَالَهُمْ فَأَرْسَلَتْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٌ ‏ ‏إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تُنَاشِدُهُ اللَّهَ وَالرَّحِمَ لَمَّا أَرْسَلَ إِلَيْهِمْ فَمَنْ أَتَاهُ فَهُوَ آمِنٌ فَأَرْسَلَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِلَيْهِمْ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ ‏ ‏حَتَّى بَلَغَ ‏ ‏حَمِيَّةَ ‏ ‏الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ ‏} ‏وَكَانَتْ ‏ ‏حَمِيَّتُهُمْ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يُقِرُّوا أَنَّهُ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ وَلَمْ يُقِرُّوا بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ وَحَالُوا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْبَيْتِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الْقَطَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ ‏ ‏وَمَرْوَانَ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏زَمَنَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏فِي بِضْعَ عَشْرَةَ مِائَةً ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَمِنْ هَاهُنَا مُلْصَقٌ بِحَدِيثِ ‏ ‏الزُّهْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏لِلْعَامِرِيِّ ‏ ‏وَمَعَهُ سَيْفُهُ إِنِّي أَرَى سَيْفَكَ هَذَا يَا أَخَا ‏ ‏بَنِي عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏جَيِّدًا قَالَ نَعَمْ أَجَلْ قَالَ أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَأَنْطَاهُ ‏ ‏إِيَّاهُ فَاسْتَلَّهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَصِيرٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ الْعَامِرِيَّ حَتَّى قَتَلَهُ وَفَرَّ ‏ ‏الْمَوْلَى ‏ ‏يَجْمِزُ ‏ ‏قِبَلَ ‏ ‏رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَدَخَلَ زَعَمُوا عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ فِي ‏ ‏الْمَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏يَطِنُّ ‏ ‏الْحَصَا مِنْ شِدَّةِ سَعْيِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حِينَ رَآهُ لَقَدْ رَأَى هَذَا ذُعْرًا فَذَكَرَ نَحْوًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَلَمَّا رَأَى ذَلِكَ كُفَّارُ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏رَكِبَ نَفَرٌ مِنْهُمْ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا إِنَّهَا لَا تُغْنِي مُدَّتُكَ شَيْئًا وَنَحْنُ نُقْتَلُ وَتُنْهَبُ أَمْوَالُنَا وَإِنَّا نَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُدْخِلَ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَسْلَمُوا مِنَّا فِي صُلْحِكَ ‏ ‏وَتَمْنَعَهُمْ وَتَحْجِزَ عَنَّا قِتَالَهُمْ فَفَعَلَ ذَلِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ { ‏وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ ‏ ‏فَقَرَأَ حَتَّى بَلَغَ ‏ ‏حَمِيَّةَ ‏ ‏الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ ‏}
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, from Maʿmar, who said: al-Zuhrī informed me, ʿUrwah ibn al-Zubayr informed me, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwān ibn al-Ḥakam — each of them confirming the other's narration — they said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ set out during the time of al-Ḥudaybiyah with over one thousand four hundred of his Companions. When they reached Dhū al-Ḥulayfah, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ garlanded the sacrificial animals, marked them, and entered the state of iḥrām for ʿumrah. He sent ahead a scout from Khuzāʿah to bring him news of Quraysh. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ marched until he reached Ghadīr al-Ashṭāṭ near ʿUsfān, and his Khuzāʿī scout came to him and said: "I left Kaʿb ibn Luʾayy and ʿĀmir ibn Luʾayy — they have gathered the Aḥābīsh and assembled forces against you. They intend to fight you and bar you from the Sacred House." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Advise me — do you think we should strike at the families of those who aided them? If they sit back, they will sit back bereaved and plundered. And if they advance, it will be a neck that Allah has severed. Or do you think we should head for the House, and whoever blocks us, we fight him?" Abū Bakr said: "Allah and His Messenger know best, O Prophet of Allah. We came only as pilgrims and did not come to fight anyone, but whoever stands between us and the House, we will fight him." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Then march on." Al-Zuhrī said — and Abū Hurayrah used to say: "I never saw anyone who consulted his Companions more than the Messenger of Allah ﷺ." Al-Zuhrī said in the narration of al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwān ibn al-Ḥakam: They marched until, when they were partway along the road, the Prophet ﷺ said: "Khālid ibn al-Walīd is at al-Ghamīm with a cavalry detachment of Quraysh as a scout." He said: "Take the right flank." By Allah, Khālid did not notice them until he saw the dust cloud of the army, and he galloped off to warn Quraysh. The Prophet ﷺ marched until he reached the pass from which one descends upon them, and his she-camel knelt down. — Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd said from Ibn al-Mubārak: "her she-camel knelt" — The Prophet ﷺ said: "Ḥal! Ḥal!" (Get up!) But she persisted. They said: "al-Qaṣwāʾ has balked!" The Prophet ﷺ said: "al-Qaṣwāʾ has not balked — that is not her nature. Rather, the One who restrained the elephant has restrained her." Then he said: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, they will not ask me for any plan in which they honor the sacred limits of Allah except that I will grant it to them." Then he urged her and she leapt up. He diverted from the main path and descended at the farthest point of al-Ḥudaybiyah by a shallow well of little water that people were scooping in small amounts. The people quickly exhausted it, and they complained to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ of thirst. He drew an arrow from his quiver and ordered them to place it in the well. By Allah, it continued gushing with abundant water until they all departed satisfied. While they were thus, Budayl ibn Warqāʾ al-Khuzāʿī came with a group of his people — they were a trusted source of sincere counsel for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ from the people of Tihāmah. He said: "I left Kaʿb ibn Luʾayy and ʿĀmir ibn Luʾayy encamped at the waters of al-Ḥudaybiyah with nursing she-camels and their young — they intend to fight you and bar you from the House." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "We did not come to fight anyone. We came only as pilgrims. War has exhausted Quraysh and harmed them. If they wish, I will make a truce with them for a period, and they shall leave a clear path between me and the people. If I prevail, then if they wish to enter what the people have entered, they may do so. Otherwise they will have rested. If they refuse — then by the One in Whose hand is my soul, I will fight them over this matter of mine until my temple is severed, or Allah will surely accomplish His command." Budayl said: "I will convey to them what you say." He went to Quraysh and said: "We have come to you from this man, and we heard him say something. If you wish, we will present it to you." Their foolish ones said: "We have no need for you to tell us anything about him." But those of sound judgment among them said: "Tell us what you heard him say." He said: "I heard him say such-and-such," and he told them what the Prophet ﷺ had said. Then ʿUrwah ibn Masʿūd al-Thaqafī stood up and said: "O my people, are you not like a father to me?" They said: "Indeed." He said: "And am I not like a son?" They said: "Indeed." He said: "Do you suspect me?" They said: "No." He said: "Do you not know that I rallied the people of ʿUkāẓ, and when they refused me, I came to you with my family and those who obeyed me?" They said: "Indeed." He said: "This man has offered you a plan of guidance — accept it, and let me go to him." They said: "Go to him." He went and began speaking with the Prophet ﷺ, who said to him words similar to what he had said to Budayl. ʿUrwah then said: "O Muḥammad, do you think — if you were to utterly destroy your people, have you ever heard of any Arab who annihilated his own people before you? And if it turns out otherwise, by Allah I see faces and I see a rabble of people fit to flee and abandon you." Abū Bakr رضي الله عنه said to him: "Go suck the clitoris of al-Lāt! Would we flee from him and abandon him?" ʿUrwah said: "Who is this?" They said: "Abū Bakr." He said: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, were it not for a favor you once did me that I have not yet repaid, I would have answered you." ʿUrwah continued speaking with the Prophet ﷺ, and every time he spoke he would take hold of the Prophet's beard. Al-Mughīrah ibn Shuʿbah was standing over the Prophet ﷺ with a sword and wearing a helmet. Every time ʿUrwah reached for the Prophet's beard, al-Mughīrah struck his hand with the flat of the sword and said: "Remove your hand from the beard of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ!" ʿUrwah raised his hand and said: "Who is this?" They said: "al-Mughīrah ibn Shuʿbah." He said: "O treacherous one! Am I not still trying to make amends for your treachery?" — al-Mughīrah had accompanied some people in the pre-Islamic period, killed them, and taken their wealth, then came and accepted Islam. The Prophet ﷺ said: "As for Islam, I accept it, but as for the wealth, I have nothing to do with it." Then ʿUrwah began gazing at the Prophet ﷺ with his eyes. By Allah, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not spit except that it fell in the palm of one of his Companions, who rubbed it on his face and skin. When he commanded them, they hastened to carry out his command. When he made ablution, they nearly fought over his ablution water. When they spoke, they lowered their voices in his presence, and they did not stare at him out of reverence. ʿUrwah returned to his companions and said: "O my people, by Allah I have visited kings — I have visited Caesar, Khosrau, and the Negus — and by Allah, I have never seen a king whose companions revere him as the Companions of Muḥammad ﷺ revere Muḥammad. By Allah, he does not spit except that it falls in the palm of one of them, and he rubs it on his face and skin. When he commands them, they hasten to carry out his command. When he makes ablution, they nearly fight over his ablution water. When they speak, they lower their voices in his presence, and they do not stare at him out of reverence. He has offered you a plan of guidance — accept it." A man from Banū Kinānah said: "Let me go to him." They said: "Go." When he came within sight of the Prophet ﷺ and his Companions, the Prophet ﷺ said: "This is so-and-so, and he is from a people who revere the sacrificial animals, so drive them out before him." They were driven out and the people met him chanting the talbiyah. When he saw that, he said: "Glory be to Allah! These people should not be barred from the House." When he returned to his companions he said: "I saw the sacrificial animals garlanded and marked. I do not see that they should be barred from the House." Then a man among them named Mikraz ibn Ḥafṣ stood and said: "Let me go to him." They said: "Go." When he came within sight, the Prophet ﷺ said: "This is Mikraz, and he is a wicked man." Mikraz began speaking with the Prophet ﷺ, and while he was speaking, Suhayl ibn ʿAmr arrived. Maʿmar said: Ayyūb informed me, from ʿIkrimah, that when Suhayl came, the Prophet ﷺ said: "Your matter has been made easy (sahula)." Al-Zuhrī said in his narration: Suhayl ibn ʿAmr came and said: "Come, let us write an agreement between us and you." The scribe was called, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Write: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful." Suhayl said: "As for al-Raḥmān, by Allah I do not know what that is" — Ibn al-Mubārak said: "what it is" — "but write: In Your name, O Allah, as you used to write." The Muslims said: "By Allah, we will not write it except as: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Write: In Your name, O Allah." Then he said: "This is what Muḥammad, the Messenger of Allah, has agreed upon." Suhayl said: "By Allah, if we knew you were the Messenger of Allah, we would not have barred you from the House or fought you. But write: Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh." The Prophet ﷺ said: "By Allah, I am indeed the Messenger of Allah even if you deny me. Write: Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh." Al-Zuhrī said: This was because of his earlier statement: "They will not ask me for any plan in which they honor Allah's sacred limits except that I will grant it to them." The Prophet ﷺ said: "On condition that you leave a clear path between us and the House so we may circumambulate it." Suhayl said: "By Allah, the Arabs will not say that we were taken by force. Rather, that shall be for you next year." So it was written. Suhayl said: "On condition that no man comes to you from us — even if he follows your religion — except that you return him to us." The Muslims said: "Glory be to Allah! How can he be returned to the polytheists when he has come as a Muslim?" While they were thus, Abū Jandal ibn Suhayl ibn ʿAmr came shuffling — Yaḥyā from Ibn al-Mubārak said: staggering — in his chains, having escaped from the lower part of Makkah and thrown himself among the Muslims. Suhayl said: "This, O Muḥammad, is the first person I demand you return to me." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "We have not yet concluded the document." Suhayl said: "Then by Allah I will never make peace with you on anything!" The Prophet ﷺ said: "Then grant him to me as an exception." He said: "I will not grant him to you." The Prophet said: "Yes, do it." He said: "I will not." Mikraz said: "Rather, we have granted him to you." Abū Jandal said: "O company of Muslims! Am I to be returned to the polytheists when I have come as a Muslim? Do you not see what I have suffered?" — and he had been severely tortured for the sake of Allah. ʿUmar رضي الله تعالى عنه said: I went to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "Are you not truly the Prophet of Allah?" He said: "Indeed." I said: "Are we not upon the truth and our enemy upon falsehood?" He said: "Indeed." I said: "Then why do we accept humiliation in our religion?" He said: "I am the Messenger of Allah and I do not disobey Him, and He is my Helper." I said: "Did you not tell us that we would come to the House and circumambulate it?" He said: "Indeed. But did I tell you that you would come to it this year?" I said: "No." He said: "Then you will come to it and circumambulate it." I went to Abū Bakr رضي الله تعالى عنه and said: "O Abū Bakr, is this not truly the Prophet of Allah?" He said: "Indeed." I said: "Are we not upon the truth and our enemy upon falsehood?" He said: "Indeed." I said: "Then why do we accept humiliation in our religion?" He said: "O man, he is the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he does not disobey his Lord عز وجل, and He is his Helper. Hold fast" — Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd said: "to his stirrup" — and he said: "cling to his stirrup until you die, for by Allah he is upon the truth." I said: "Did he not tell us we would come to the House and circumambulate it?" He said: "Indeed. But did he tell you that you would come to it this year?" I said: "No." He said: "Then you will come to it and circumambulate it." Al-Zuhrī said: ʿUmar said: "I did deeds to atone for that." When the treaty document was concluded, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to his Companions: "Rise, slaughter your sacrificial animals, and then shave your heads." By Allah, not one of them stood up, even though he said it three times. When none of them stood, he entered upon Umm Salamah and mentioned to her the people's response. Umm Salamah said: "O Messenger of Allah, do you wish that? Go out and do not speak a word to any of them until you have slaughtered your sacrificial animal and called your barber to shave you." He went out and did not speak to any of them until he had done that — he slaughtered his sacrificial animal and called his barber. When they saw that, they rose and slaughtered, and some of them began shaving others until they nearly killed one another out of grief. Then believing women came to him, and Allah عز وجل revealed: {O you who believe, when believing women come to you as emigrants, examine them} until {nor the ties of the disbelieving women}. ʿUmar divorced two wives of his that day who had been his in the pre-Islamic period. One of them was married by Muʿāwiyah ibn Abī Sufyān and the other by Ṣafwān ibn Umayyah. Then the Prophet ﷺ returned to Madinah, and Abū Baṣīr came to him — a man from Quraysh who was Muslim. — Yaḥyā from Ibn al-Mubārak said: Abū Baṣīr ibn Usayd al-Thaqafī came to him as a Muslim emigrant — al-Akhnas ibn Sharīq hired a disbelieving man from Banū ʿĀmir ibn Luʾayy and a freed slave with him, and wrote with them to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ requesting fulfillment of the treaty. They sent two men to retrieve him, saying: "The covenant you made with us." So he handed him over to the two men. They took him out until they reached Dhū al-Ḥulayfah, and they stopped to eat from their dates. Abū Baṣīr said to one of the two men: "By Allah, I see your sword is excellent, O so-and-so." The other drew it and said: "Indeed, by Allah it is excellent — I have tested it, then tested it again." Abū Baṣīr said: "Let me look at it." He handed it to him, and Abū Baṣīr struck him until he was dead. The other fled until he reached Madinah and entered the mosque running. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "This man has seen something terrifying." When he reached the Prophet ﷺ, he said: "By Allah, my companion has been killed, and I am as good as killed!" Then Abū Baṣīr came and said: "O Prophet of Allah, by Allah, Allah has fulfilled your obligation — you returned me to them, and then Allah rescued me from them." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Woe to his mother — he would be a kindler of war if only he had someone with him!" When Abū Baṣīr heard that, he knew the Prophet would send him back to them, so he left and went to the seacoast. Abū Jandal ibn Suhayl escaped and joined Abū Baṣīr. Thereafter, no man from Quraysh who had accepted Islam left except that he joined Abū Baṣīr, until they formed a band. By Allah, whenever they heard of a caravan of Quraysh heading for Syria, they intercepted it, killed them, and took their wealth. Quraysh sent word to the Prophet ﷺ, imploring him by Allah and by kinship to send for them — whoever came to him would be safe. So the Prophet ﷺ sent for them. Allah عز وجل revealed: {And it is He who restrained their hands from you and your hands from them} until {the zealotry of the Age of Ignorance}. Their zealotry was that they did not acknowledge him as the Prophet of Allah, did not acknowledge "In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful," and barred him from the House. Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd al-Qaṭṭān narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd Allāh ibn al-Mubārak narrated to us, Maʿmar narrated to us, from al-Zuhrī, from ʿUrwah, from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah and Marwān ibn al-Ḥakam, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ set out during the time of al-Ḥudaybiyah with over one thousand four hundred — and he mentioned the hadith. He said: And from here it is appended to the narration of al-Zuhrī from al-Qāsim ibn Muḥammad, who said: Abū Baṣīr said to the ʿĀmirī — who had his sword with him — "I see your sword is excellent, O brother of Banū ʿĀmir." He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "Let me look at it." So he handed it to him, and Abū Baṣīr drew it and struck the ʿĀmirī until he killed him. The freed slave fled running toward the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and entered — they claim — upon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was in the mosque, the pebbles rattling from the speed of his running. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said when he saw him: "This man has seen something terrifying." He mentioned a narration similar to that of ʿAbd al-Razzāq. He said: When the disbelievers of Quraysh saw that, a group of them rode to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "Your truce is of no use to us while we are being killed and our wealth plundered. We ask you to include these Muslims among us in your peace agreement, protect them, and prevent them from fighting us." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ did so, and Allah عز وجل revealed: {And it is He who restrained their hands from you and your hands from them} — and he recited until {the zealotry of the Age of Ignorance}.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبَّادٍ الْمَكِّيُّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى ‏ ‏بَنِي هَاشِمٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أُمِّ بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏وَجَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي رَافِعٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَ ‏ ‏حَسَنُ بْنُ حَسَنٍ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرِ ‏ ‏يَخْطُبُ بِنْتًا لَهُ قَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏تُوَافِينِي فِي ‏ ‏الْعَتَمَةِ ‏ ‏فَلَقِيَهُ فَحَمِدَ اللَّهَ ‏ ‏الْمِسْوَرُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ مَا مِنْ سَبَبٍ وَلَا نَسَبٍ وَلَا صِهْرٍ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ نَسَبِكُمْ وَصِهْرِكُمْ وَلَكِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَاطِمَةُ ‏ ‏شُجْنَةٌ ‏ ‏مِنِّي يَبْسُطُنِي مَا بَسَطَهَا وَيَقْبِضُنِي مَا قَبَضَهَا وَإِنَّهُ يَنْقَطِعُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ الْأَنْسَابُ وَالْأَسْبَابُ إِلَّا نَسَبِي وَسَبَبِي وَتَحْتَكَ ابْنَتُهَا وَلَوْ زَوَّجْتُكَ قَبَضَهَا ذَلِكَ فَذَهَبَ عَاذِرًا لَهُ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn ʿAbbād al-Makkī narrated to us, Abū Saʿīd — the freed slave of Banū Hāshim — narrated to us, ʿAbd Allāh ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, from Umm Bakr and Jaʿfar, from ʿUbayd Allāh ibn Abī Rāfiʿ, from al-Miswar, who said: Ḥasan ibn Ḥasan sent word to al-Miswar proposing marriage to one of his daughters. Al-Miswar said to him: "Meet me at the ʿatamah (late night) prayer." He met him, and al-Miswar praised Allah, then said: "There is no tie of kinship, lineage, or marriage more beloved to me than your kinship and marriage ties. But the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Fāṭimah is an offshoot of me; what makes her happy makes me happy, and what grieves her grieves me. On the Day of Resurrection all ties of lineage and kinship will be severed except my lineage and my ties. Your wife is her daughter, and if I were to marry you [my daughter], it would grieve her.'" The man departed excusing him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَجَّاجُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ ‏ ‏لَيْثٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏بَكْرٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْأَشَجِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَابِلٍ ‏ ‏صَاحِبِ الْعَبَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ صَاحِبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ مَرَرْتُ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَسَلَّمْتُ ‏ ‏فَرَدَّ إِلَيَّ إِشَارَةً وَقَالَ لَا أَعْلَمُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ إِشَارَةً بِإِصْبَعِهِ ‏
en: Ḥajjāj ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, he said: Layth — meaning ibn Saʿd — said, Bakr — meaning ibn ʿAbd Allāh ibn al-Ashajj — narrated to me, from Nābil Ṣāḥib al-ʿAbāʾ, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿUmar, from Ṣuhayb, the Companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that he said: I passed by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was praying. I greeted him, and he returned my greeting with a gesture. He said: "I do not know except that he said: a gesture with his finger."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏مَنْ ‏ ‏النَّمِرِ بْنِ قَاسِطٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبَ بْنَ سِنَانٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏أَصْدَقَ ‏ ‏امْرَأَةً ‏ ‏صَدَاقًا ‏ ‏وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ لَا يُرِيدُ أَدَاءَهُ إِلَيْهَا فَغَرَّهَا بِاللَّهِ وَاسْتَحَلَّ فَرْجَهَا بِالْبَاطِلِ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ يَوْمَ يَلْقَاهُ وَهُوَ زَانٍ وَأَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ ادَّانَ مِنْ رَجُلٍ دَيْنًا وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ لَا يُرِيدُ أَدَاءَهُ إِلَيْهِ فَغَرَّهُ بِاللَّهِ وَاسْتَحَلَّ مَالَهُ بِالْبَاطِلِ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يَوْمَ يَلْقَاهُ وَهُوَ سَارِقٌ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, ʿAbd al-Ḥamīd ibn Jaʿfar informed us, from al-Ḥasan ibn Muḥammad al-Anṣārī, who said: A man from al-Namir ibn Qāsiṭ narrated to me, he said: I heard Ṣuhayb ibn Sinān narrating. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Any man who pledges a dowry to a woman while Allah knows that he does not intend to pay it to her — he has deceived her by invoking Allah and has taken her private parts through falsehood. He will meet Allah on the Day he meets Him as an adulterer. And any man who borrows a debt from another while Allah knows that he does not intend to repay it — he has deceived him by invoking Allah and has taken his wealth through falsehood. He will meet Allah عز وجل on the Day he meets Him as a thief."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُحَرِّكُ شَفَتَيْهِ أَيَّامَ ‏ ‏حُنَيْنٍ ‏ ‏بِشَيْءٍ لَمْ يَكُنْ يَفْعَلُهُ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ نَبِيًّا كَانَ فِيمَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ أَعْجَبَتْهُ أُمَّتُهُ فَقَالَ لَنْ ‏ ‏يَرُومَ ‏ ‏هَؤُلَاءِ شَيْءٌ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ أَنْ خَيِّرْهُمْ بَيْنَ إِحْدَى ثَلَاثٍ إِمَّا أَنْ أُسَلِّطَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَدُوًّا مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ فَيَسْتَبِيحَهُمْ أَوْ الْجُوعَ أَوْ الْمَوْتَ قَالَ فَقَالُوا أَمَّا الْقَتْلُ أَوْ الْجُوعُ فَلَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ وَلَكِنْ الْمَوْتُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَمَاتَ فِي ثَلَاثٍ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفًا قَالَ فَقَالَ فَأَنَا أَقُولُ الْآنَ اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أُحَاوِلُ وَبِكَ ‏ ‏أَصُولُ ‏ ‏وَبِكَ أُقَاتِلُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from Thābit, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to move his lips during the days of Ḥunayn with something he had not been doing before that. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Indeed, a prophet among those before you was impressed by the great numbers of his ummah and said: 'Nothing shall challenge these.' So Allah revealed to him: 'Give them a choice of three: either I unleash upon them an enemy from others who will devastate them, or hunger, or death.' They said: 'We cannot bear being killed or hunger, but death.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Seventy thousand of them died in three days.' He said: 'So now I say: O Allah, by You I strive, by You I attack, and by You I fight.'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏وَحَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏قَالَا ثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَجِبْتُ مِنْ أَمْرِ الْمُؤْمِنِ إِنَّ أَمْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِ كُلَّهُ لَهُ خَيْرٌ وَلَيْسَ ذَلِكَ لِأَحَدٍ إِلَّا لِلْمُؤْمِنِ إِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ ‏ ‏سَرَّاءُ ‏ ‏شَكَرَ كَانَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ خَيْرًا وَإِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ ضَرَّاءُ فَصَبَرَ كَانَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ خَيْرًا ‏
en: Bahz and Ḥajjāj narrated to us, they said: Sulaymān ibn al-Mughīrah narrated to us, from Thābit, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "How wondrous is the affair of the believer! Indeed, the entire affair of the believer is good for him, and that is not for anyone except the believer. If prosperity befalls him, he is grateful, and that is good for him. If adversity befalls him, he is patient, and that is good for him."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا دَخَلَ أَهْلُ الْجَنَّةِ الْجَنَّةَ نُودُوا يَا أَهْلَ الْجَنَّةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ مَوْعِدًا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ لَمْ تَرَوْهُ فَقَالُوا وَمَا هُوَ أَلَمْ تُبَيِّضْ وُجُوهَنَا وَتُزَحْزِحْنَا عَنْ النَّارِ وَتُدْخِلْنَا الْجَنَّةَ قَالَ فَيُكْشَفُ الْحِجَابُ فَيَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْهِ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا أَعْطَاهُمْ اللَّهُ شَيْئًا أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ تَلَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ { ‏لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَى وَزِيَادَةٌ ‏}
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah informed us, from Thābit al-Bunānī, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the people of Paradise enter Paradise, they will be called: 'O people of Paradise! You have a promise with Allah that He has not yet shown you.' They will say: 'What is it? Has He not brightened our faces, saved us from the Fire, and admitted us to Paradise?' Then the veil will be lifted, and they will gaze upon Him. By Allah, Allah never gave them anything more beloved to them than gazing upon Him." Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited: {For those who have done good is the best reward and more} [10:26].

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ثَابِتٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا دَخَلَ أَهْلُ الْجَنَّةِ الْجَنَّةَ وَأَهْلُ النَّارِ النَّارَ نُودُوا يَا أَهْلَ الْجَنَّةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَوْعِدًا فَقَالُوا أَلَمْ يُثَقِّلْ مَوَازِينَنَا ‏ ‏وَيُعْطِينَا كُتُبَنَا بِأَيْمَانِنَا وَيُدْخِلْنَا الْجَنَّةَ ‏ ‏وَيُنْجِينَا مِنْ النَّارِ فَيُكْشَفُ الْحِجَابُ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَيَتَجَلَّى اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَهُمْ قَالَ فَمَا أَعْطَاهُمْ اللَّهُ شَيْئًا أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ النَّظَرِ إِلَيْهِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Thābit, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "When the people of Paradise enter Paradise and the people of the Fire enter the Fire, a caller will announce: 'O people of Paradise! You have a promise with Allah that He wishes to fulfill for you.' They will say: 'What is it? Has He not made our scales heavy, brightened our faces, admitted us to Paradise, and saved us from the Fire?' Then the veil will be lifted and they will gaze upon Him. By Allah, He never gave them anything more beloved to them than gazing upon Him."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏مِنْ كِتَابِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْمُغِيرَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا صَلَّى هَمَسَ شَيْئًا لَا نَفْهَمُهُ وَلَا يُحَدِّثُنَا بِهِ قَالَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَطِنْتُمْ ‏ ‏لِي قَالَ قَائِلٌ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَإِنِّي قَدْ ذَكَرْتُ نَبِيًّا مِنْ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ أُعْطِيَ جُنُودًا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ فَقَالَ مَنْ يُكَافِئُ هَؤُلَاءِ ‏ ‏أَوْ مَنْ يَقُومُ لِهَؤُلَاءِ أَوْ كَلِمَةً شَبِيهَةً بِهَذِهِ شَكَّ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ اخْتَرْ لِقَوْمِكَ بَيْنَ إِحْدَى ثَلَاثٍ إِمَّا أَنْ أُسَلِّطَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَدُوًّا مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ أَوْ الْجُوعَ أَوْ الْمَوْتَ قَالَ فَاسْتَشَارَ قَوْمَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ فَقَالُوا أَنْتَ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏نَكِلُ ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ إِلَيْكَ فَخِرْ لَنَا قَالَ فَقَامَ إِلَى صَلَاتِهِ قَالَ وَكَانُوا يَفْزَعُونَ إِذَا فَزِعُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ قَالَ فَصَلَّى قَالَ أَمَّا عَدُوٌّ مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ فَلَا أَوْ الْجُوعُ فَلَا وَلَكِنْ الْمَوْتُ قَالَ فَسُلِّطَ عَلَيْهِمْ الْمَوْتُ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ فَمَاتَ مِنْهُمْ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفًا فَهَمْسِي الَّذِي تَرَوْنَ أَنِّي أَقُولُ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ يَا رَبِّ بِكَ أُقَاتِلُ وَبِكَ ‏ ‏أُصَاوِلُ ‏ ‏وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏بِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ سَوَاءً بِهَذَا الْكَلَامِ كُلِّهِ ‏ ‏وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ وَلَمْ يَقُلْ فِيهِ كَانُوا إِذَا فَزِعُوا فَزِعُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us from his book, he said: Sulaymān — meaning ibn al-Mughīrah — narrated to us, Thābit narrated to us, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, whenever he prayed, used to whisper something we could not understand, and he did not tell us about it. He said: "Have you noticed me?" Someone said: "Yes." He said: "I was recalling a prophet among the prophets who was given armies from his people and said: 'Who can match these?' — or 'Who can stand against these?' — or a similar word" — Sulaymān was uncertain. "Allah revealed to him: 'Choose for your people one of three: either I unleash upon them an enemy from others, or hunger, or death.' He consulted his people, and they said: 'You are the Prophet of Allah; we entrust it to you, so choose for us.' He stood to pray" — he said: "and they would flee to prayer whenever they were alarmed" — "He prayed, then said: 'Not an enemy from others, nor hunger, but death.' So death was unleashed upon them for three days, and seventy thousand of them died. My whispering that you see is me saying: O Allah, O Lord, by You I fight, by You I attack, and there is no power or strength except by Allah." ʿAffān narrated to us, he said: Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us with this same hadith — all of it with this same isnād and wording — except that he did not include: "they would flee to prayer whenever they were alarmed."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏مِنْ كِتَابِهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَجِبْتُ لِأَمْرِ الْمُؤْمِنِ ‏ ‏إِنَّ أَمْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِ كُلَّهُ لَهُ خَيْرٌ لَيْسَ ذَلِكَ لِأَحَدٍ إِلَّا لِلْمُؤْمِنِ إِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ سَرَّاءُ شَكَرَ وَكَانَ خَيْرًا وَإِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ ضَرَّاءُ صَبَرَ وَكَانَ خَيْرًا ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us from his book, Sulaymān narrated to us, Thābit narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I am amazed at the affair of the believer. The entire affair of the believer is good for him; that is not for anyone except the believer. If prosperity befalls him, he is grateful, and it is good for him. If adversity befalls him, he is patient, and it is good for him."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ أَيَّامَ ‏ ‏حُنَيْنٍ ‏ ‏يُحَرِّكُ شَفَتَيْهِ بَعْدَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ بِشَيْءٍ لَمْ نَكُنْ ‏ ‏نَرَاهُ يَفْعَلُهُ فَقُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا نَرَاكَ تَفْعَلُ شَيْئًا لَمْ تَكُنْ تَفْعَلُهُ فَمَا هَذَا الَّذِي تُحَرِّكُ شَفَتَيْكَ قَالَ إِنَّ نَبِيًّا فِيمَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ أَعْجَبَتْهُ كَثْرَةُ أُمَّتِهِ فَقَالَ لَنْ يَرُومَ هَؤُلَاءِ شَيْءٌ فَأَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ أَنْ خَيِّرْ أُمَّتَكَ بَيْنَ إِحْدَى ثَلَاثٍ إِمَّا أَنْ نُسَلِّطَ عَلَيْهِمْ عَدُوًّا مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ فَيَسْتَبِيحَهُمْ أَوْ الْجُوعَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ أُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ الْمَوْتَ فَشَاوَرَهُمْ فَقَالُوا أَمَّا الْعَدُوُّ فَلَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِمْ وَأَمَّا الْجُوعُ فَلَا صَبْرَ لَنَا عَلَيْهِ وَلَكِنْ الْمَوْتُ فَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ الْمَوْتَ فَمَاتَ مِنْهُمْ فِي ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفًا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَنَا أَقُولُ الْآنَ حَيْثُ رَأَى كَثْرَتَهُمْ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ ‏ ‏أُحَاوِلُ ‏ ‏وَبِكَ ‏ ‏أُصَاوِلُ ‏ ‏وَبِكَ أُقَاتِلُ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Ḥammād — meaning ibn Salamah — narrated to us, Thābit narrated to us, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to move his lips during the days of Ḥunayn after the Fajr prayer with something they had not seen him do before. They said: "O Messenger of Allah, we see you doing something you did not used to do — what is this that you move your lips with?" He said: "A prophet among those before you was impressed by the great numbers of his ummah and said: 'Nothing shall challenge these.' So Allah revealed to him: 'Give your ummah a choice of three: either I unleash upon them an enemy from others who will devastate them, or hunger, or that I send upon them death.' He consulted them, and they said: 'As for the enemy, we have no strength for them. As for hunger, we have no patience for it. But death.' So death was sent upon them, and seventy thousand of them died in three days. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'So now I say — when he saw their great numbers — O Allah, by You I strive, by You I attack, and by You I fight.'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ثَابِتٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏صُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَلَا هَذِهِ ‏ ‏الْآيَةَ ‏ { ‏لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَى وَزِيَادَةٌ ‏} ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا دَخَلَ أَهْلُ الْجَنَّةِ الْجَنَّةَ وَأَهْلُ النَّارِ النَّارَ نَادَى مُنَادٍ يَا أَهْلَ الْجَنَّةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَوْعِدًا يُرِيدُ أَنْ ‏ ‏يُنْجِزَكُمُوهُ ‏ ‏فَيَقُولُونَ وَمَا هُوَ أَلَمْ يُثَقِّلْ مَوَازِينَنَا وَيُبَيِّضْ وُجُوهَنَا وَيُدْخِلْنَا الْجَنَّةَ وَيُجِرْنَا مِنْ النَّارِ قَالَ فَيُكْشَفُ لَهُمْ الْحِجَابُ فَيَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْهِ قَالَ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا أَعْطَاهُمْ شَيْئًا أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ النَّظَرِ إِلَيْهِ وَلَا أَقَرَّ بِأَعْيُنِهِمْ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Ḥammād informed us, Thābit informed us, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from Ṣuhayb, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited this verse: {For those who have done good is the best reward and more} [10:26]. He said: "When the people of Paradise enter Paradise and the people of the Fire enter the Fire, a caller will announce: 'O people of Paradise! You have a promise with Allah that He wishes to fulfill for you.' They will say: 'What is it? Has He not made our scales heavy, brightened our faces, admitted us to Paradise, and saved us from the Fire?' Then the veil will be lifted and they will gaze upon Him. By Allah, He never gave them anything more beloved to them than gazing upon Him, nor anything more pleasing to their eyes."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لِصُهَيْبٍ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا ‏ ‏لَوْلَا ثَلَاثُ ‏ ‏خِصَالٍ ‏ ‏فِيكَ لَمْ يَكُنْ بِكَ بَأْسٌ قَالَ وَمَا هُنَّ فَوَاللَّهِ مَا نَرَاكَ تَعِيبُ شَيْئًا قَالَ اكْتِنَاؤُكَ ‏ ‏بِأَبِي يَحْيَى ‏ ‏وَلَيْسَ لَكَ وَلَدٌ وَادِّعَاؤُكَ إِلَى ‏ ‏النَّمِرِ بْنِ قَاسِطٍ ‏ ‏وَأَنْتَ رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏أَلْكَنُ ‏ ‏وَأَنَّكَ لَا تُمْسِكُ الْمَالَ قَالَ أَمَّا اكْتِنَائِي ‏ ‏بِأَبِي يَحْيَى ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَنَّانِي بِهَا فَلَا أَدَعُهَا حَتَّى أَلْقَاهُ وَأَمَّا ادِّعَائِي إِلَى ‏ ‏النَّمِرِ بْنِ قَاسِطٍ ‏ ‏فَإِنِّي امْرُؤٌ مِنْهُمْ وَلَكِنْ اسْتُرْضِعَ لِي ‏ ‏بِالْأَيْلَةِ ‏ ‏فَهَذِهِ ‏ ‏اللُّكْنَةُ ‏ ‏مِنْ ذَاكَ وَأَمَّا الْمَالُ فَهَلْ تُرَانِي أُنْفِقُ إِلَّا فِي حَقٍّ ‏
en: Bahz narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, Zayd ibn Aslam informed us, that ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb said to Ṣuhayb رضي الله عنهما: "Were it not for three traits in you, there would be nothing wrong with you." He said: "What are they? By Allah, we do not see you finding fault with anything." He said: "Your taking the kunyah Abū Yaḥyā when you have no son, your claiming lineage to al-Namir ibn Qāsiṭ when you are a man with a speech impediment, and your inability to hold onto wealth." He said: "As for my kunyah of Abū Yaḥyā — the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me that kunyah, and I shall not abandon it until I meet him. As for my claiming lineage to al-Namir ibn Qāsiṭ — I am indeed a man of their lineage, but I was sent as a nursling to Aylah, and this speech impediment is from that. As for wealth — do you see me spending except in what is right?"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَاجِيَةَ الْخُزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ صَاحِبَ بُدْنِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ كَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ بِمَا ‏ ‏عَطِبَ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْبُدْنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏انْحَرْهُ وَاغْمِسْ نَعْلَهُ فِي دَمِهِ وَاضْرِبْ صَفْحَتَهُ ‏ ‏وَخَلِّ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ النَّاسِ وَبَيْنَهُ فَلْيَأْكُلُوهُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Hishām ibn ʿUrwah narrated to us, from his father, from Nājiyah al-Khuzāʿī — and he was the keeper of the sacrificial camels of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — who said: I said: "How should I deal with those of the sacrificial camels that become injured?" He said: "Slaughter it, dip its sandal-marker in its blood, strike its flank with it, and leave it for the people to eat."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَاجِيَةَ الْخُزَاعِيِّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ صَاحِبَ بُدْنِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ بِمَا عَطِبَ مِنْ الْإِبِلِ ‏ ‏أَوْ الْبُدْنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏انْحَرْهَا ثُمَّ أَلْقِ نَعْلَهَا فِي دَمِهَا ثُمَّ ‏ ‏خَلِّ ‏ ‏عَنْهَا وَعَنْ النَّاسِ فَلْيَأْكُلُوهَا ‏
en: Abū Muʿāwiyah narrated to us, Hishām ibn ʿUrwah narrated to us, from his father, from Nājiyah al-Khuzāʿī — and he was the keeper of the sacrificial camels of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — who said: I said: "O Messenger of Allah, how should I deal with those of the camels — or the sacrificial animals — that become injured?" He said: "Slaughter it, then cast its sandal-marker in its blood, then leave it and let the people eat it."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو عَبْد الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَكَتَبَ بِهِ إِلَىَّ ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْكَ بِخَطِّي وَخَتَمْتُ الْكِتَابَ بِخَاتَمِي وَنَقْشُهُ اللَّهُ وَلِيُّ ‏ ‏سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ وَهُوَ خَاتَمُ أَبِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏لَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَعْفَرِ بْنِ رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بَكْرِ بْنِ سَوَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُسْلِمِ بْنِ مَخْشِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْفِرَاسِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏الْفِرَاسِيَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَسْأَلُ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا وَإِنْ كُنْتَ سَائِلًا لَا بُدَّ فَاسْأَلْ الصَّالِحِينَ ‏
en: Qutaybah ibn Saʿīd narrated to us — Abū ʿAbd al-Raḥmān said: and Qutaybah ibn Saʿīd wrote to me: "I have written to you in my own hand and sealed the letter with my seal, whose inscription reads 'Allah is the protector of Saʿīd, may Allah have mercy on him,' and it is the seal of my father" — Layth ibn Saʿd narrated to us, from Jaʿfar ibn Rabīʿah, from Bakr ibn Sawādah, from Muslim ibn Makhshī, from Ibn al-Firāsī, that al-Firāsī said to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "Shall I beg?" The Prophet ﷺ said: "No. But if you must beg, then ask the righteous."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏وَكَتَبَ بِهِ إِلَيَّ ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏لَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَا مُوسَى الْغَافِقِيَّ ‏ ‏سَمِعَ ‏ ‏عُقْبَةَ بْنَ عَامِرٍ الْجُهَنِيَّ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ ‏ ‏عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَحَادِيثَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو مُوسَى ‏ ‏إِنَّ صَاحِبَكُمْ هَذَا لَحَافِظٌ أَوْ هَالِكٌ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ آخِرُ مَا ‏ ‏عَهِدَ ‏ ‏إِلَيْنَا أَنْ قَالَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْكُمْ بِكِتَابِ اللَّهِ وَسَتَرْجِعُونَ إِلَى قَوْمٍ يُحِبُّونَ الْحَدِيثَ عَنِّي فَمَنْ قَالَ عَلَيَّ مَا لَمْ أَقُلْ ‏ ‏فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ النَّارِ وَمَنْ حِفْظَ عَنِّي شَيْئًا فَلْيُحَدِّثْهُ ‏
en: Qutaybah ibn Saʿīd narrated to us — and Qutaybah wrote it to me — Layth ibn Saʿd narrated to us, from ʿAmr ibn al-Ḥārith, from Yaḥyā ibn Maymūn al-Ḥaḍramī, that Abū Mūsā al-Ghāfiqī heard ʿUqbah ibn ʿĀmir al-Juhanī narrating on the pulpit about the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — several hadiths. Abū Mūsā said: "This companion of yours is either a precise memorizer or doomed. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, among the last things he instructed us with, said: 'Hold fast to the Book of Allah, and you will return to a people who love narrating hadith from me. Whoever attributes to me what I did not say, let him take his seat in the Fire. And whoever memorizes something from me, let him narrate it.'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْعُشَرَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَمَا تَكُونُ ‏ ‏الذَّكَاةُ ‏ ‏إِلَّا فِي ‏ ‏الْحَلْقِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏اللَّبَّةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَوْ طَعَنْتَ فِي فَخِذِهَا لَأَجْزَأَكَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الْعُشَرَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِثْلَهُ قَالَ وَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ وَأَبِيكَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَاه ‏ ‏هُدْبَةُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْحَجَّاجِ ‏ ‏قَالَا ثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْعُشَرَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِثْلَ حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏وَكِيعٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَوْثَرَةُ بْنُ أَشْرَسَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ نَحْوَهُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Abū al-ʿUsharāʾ, from his father, who said: I said: "O Messenger of Allah, is slaughtering only valid at the throat or the upper chest?" He said: "Even if you pierced its thigh, it would suffice for you." ʿAffān narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Abū al-ʿUsharāʾ, from his father, from the Prophet ﷺ — a similar narration. He said: And I heard him say: "By your father."
