Hudbah ibn Khālid and Ibrāhīm ibn al-Ḥajjāj narrated to us, they said: Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, Abū al-ʿUsharāʾ narrated to us, from his father, from the Prophet ﷺ — like the hadith of Wakīʿ. Ḥawthara ibn Ashras narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us — and he mentioned a similar narration.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏وَكَتَبَ بِهِ إِلَيَّ ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُجَمِّعُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مُجَمِّعٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قِيلَ ‏ ‏لِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبَةَ ‏ ‏مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَدْ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدِمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ غُلَامٌ حَدِيثٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ جَاءَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَوْمًا إِلَى مَسْجِدِنَا ‏ ‏يَعْنِي مَسْجِدَ ‏ ‏قُبَاءَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَجِئْنَا فَجَلَسْنَا إِلَيْهِ وَجَلَسَ إِلَيْهِ النَّاسُ قَالَ فَجَلَسَ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ أَنْ يَجْلِسَ ثُمَّ قَامَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَرَأَيْتُهُ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فِي نَعْلَيْهِ ‏
en: Qutaybah ibn Saʿīd narrated to us — and Qutaybah wrote it to me — Mujammiʿ ibn Yaʿqūb narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Ismāʿīl ibn Mujammiʿ, who said: ʿAbd Allāh ibn Abī Ḥabībah was asked: "What do you remember of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" — and he had been alive when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ arrived, as a young boy. He said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to us one day at our mosque — meaning the mosque of Qubāʾ. We came and sat with him, and the people sat with him. He sat as long as Allah willed, then he stood to pray. I saw him praying in his sandals."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَطَّافُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُجَمِّعُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏غُلَامٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏قُبَاءَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ أَدْرَكَهُ شَيْخًا قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِقُبَاءَ ‏ ‏فَجَلَسَ فِي فِنَاءِ الْأُجُمِ وَاجْتَمَعَ إِلَيْهِ نَاسٌ فَاسْتَسْقَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَسُقِيَ فَشَرِبَ وَأَنَا عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَأَنَا أَحْدَثُ الْقَوْمِ فَنَاوَلَنِي فَشَرِبْتُ وَحَفِظْتُ أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏صَلَّى بِنَا يَوْمَئِذٍ وَعَلَيْهِ نَعْلَانِ لَمْ يَنْزِعْهُمَا ‏
en: Yūnus ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, al-ʿAṭṭāf narrated to us, Mujammiʿ ibn Yaʿqūb narrated to me, from a boy from the people of Qubāʾ whom he met as an old man, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to us at Qubāʾ and sat in the courtyard of the fortified tower, and people gathered around him. He asked for something to drink, and he drank. I was at his right side, and I was the youngest of the group, so he handed it to me and I drank. I also remember that he prayed with us that day wearing his sandals, which he did not remove.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وسَمِعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الدَّرَاوَرْدِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَنَا النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَصَلَّى بِنَا فِي مَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏بَنِي عَبْدِ الْأَشْهَلِ ‏ ‏فَرَأَيْتُهُ وَاضِعًا ‏ ‏يَدَهُ فِي ‏ ‏ثَوْبِهِ إِذَا سَجَدَ ‏
en: ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah narrated to us — ʿAbd Allāh said: and I also heard it from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah — ʿAbd al-ʿAzīz ibn Muḥammad al-Darāwardī narrated to us, from Ismāʿīl ibn Abī Ḥabībah, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, who said: The Prophet ﷺ came to us and prayed with us in the mosque of Banū ʿAbd al-Ashhal. I saw him placing his hand inside his garment when he prostrated.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَطَاءٍ اللَّيْثِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ يَعْمَرَ الدِّيلِيَّ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏شَهِدْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ وَاقِفٌ ‏ ‏بِعَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏فَأَتَاهُ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏نَجْدٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَيْفَ الْحَجُّ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْحَجُّ حَجُّ ‏ ‏عَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏مَنْ جَاءَ قَبْلَ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ مِنْ لَيْلَةِ ‏ ‏جَمْعٍ ‏ ‏تَمَّ حَجُّهُ أَيَّامُ ‏ ‏مِنًى ‏ ‏ثَلَاثَةٌ ‏ { ‏فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَنْ تَأَخَّرَ فَلَا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ‏} ‏ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَرْدَفَ ‏ ‏خَلْفَهُ فَجَعَلَ يُنَادِي بِهِنَّ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Bukayr ibn ʿAṭāʾ al-Laythī, who said: I heard ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Yaʿmar al-Dīlī saying: I witnessed the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while he was standing at ʿArafah. People from the people of Najd came to him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, how is the Ḥajj performed?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Ḥajj is ʿArafah. Whoever arrives before the Fajr prayer on the night of Jamʿ (Muzdalifah), his Ḥajj is complete. The days of Minā are three: {whoever hastens in two days, there is no sin upon him, and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him} [2:203]." Then he seated someone behind him on his mount, and that person began calling out these instructions.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُرَيْجٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ زَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بِشْرِ بْنِ سُحَيْمٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ أَنْ يُنَادَى أَيَّامَ ‏ ‏التَّشْرِيقِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَا يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مُؤْمِنٌ وَهِيَ أَيَّامُ أَكْلٍ وَشُرْبٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَبِيبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بِشْرِ بْنِ سُحَيْمٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَطَبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي أَيَّامِ ‏ ‏التَّشْرِيقِ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ نَحْوَهُ وَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ هَذِهِ أَيَّامُ أَكْلٍ وَشُرْبٍ ‏
en: Surayj narrated to us, Ḥammād — meaning ibn Zayd — narrated to us, from ʿAmr ibn Dīnār, from Nāfiʿ ibn Jubayr, from Bishr ibn Suḥaym, that the Prophet ﷺ ordered a proclamation to be made during the days of Tashrīq: that none shall enter Paradise except a believer, and that these are days of eating and drinking. ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ḥabīb, from Nāfiʿ ibn Jubayr, from Bishr ibn Suḥaym, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon during the days of Tashrīq — and he mentioned a similar narration — and said: "These are days of eating and drinking."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏وَسَمِعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ الْمَعَافِرِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ بِشْرٍ الْخَثْعَمِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏سَمِعَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏لَتُفْتَحَنَّ ‏ ‏الْقُسْطَنْطِينِيَّةُ ‏ ‏فَلَنِعْمَ الْأَمِيرُ أَمِيرُهَا وَلَنِعْمَ الْجَيْشُ ذَلِكَ الْجَيْشُ قَالَ فَدَعَانِي ‏ ‏مَسْلَمَةُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ ‏ ‏فَسَأَلَنِي فَحَدَّثْتُهُ فَغَزَا ‏ ‏الْقُسْطَنْطِينِيَّةَ ‏
en: ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah narrated to us — and I also heard it from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah — he said: Zayd ibn al-Ḥubāb narrated to us, he said: al-Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah al-Maʿāfirī narrated to me, he said: ʿAbd Allāh ibn Bishr al-Khathʿamī narrated to me, from his father, that he heard the Prophet ﷺ saying: "Constantinople shall surely be conquered. What an excellent leader its leader will be, and what an excellent army that army will be." He said: Maslamah ibn ʿAbd al-Malik summoned me and asked me, so I narrated it to him, and he launched an expedition against Constantinople.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَسَمِعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ الْفَزَارِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الطَّائِفِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْعَدْوَانِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏أَبْصَرَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي مَشْرِقِ ‏ ‏ثَقِيفٍ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ عَلَى قَوْسٍ أَوْ عَصًا حِينَ أَتَاهُمْ ‏ ‏يَبْتَغِي ‏ ‏عِنْدَهُمْ النَّصْرَ قَالَ فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقْرَأُ ‏ ‏وَالسَّمَاءِ وَالطَّارِقِ ‏ ‏حَتَّى خَتَمَهَا قَالَ فَوَعَيْتُهَا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَأَنَا مُشْرِكٌ ثُمَّ قَرَأْتُهَا فِي الْإِسْلَامِ قَالَ فَدَعَتْنِي ‏ ‏ثَقِيفٌ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا مَاذَا سَمِعْتَ مِنْ هَذَا الرَّجُلِ فَقَرَأْتُهَا عَلَيْهِمْ فَقَالَ مَنْ مَعَهُمْ مِنْ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشٍ ‏ ‏نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِصَاحِبِنَا لَوْ كُنَّا نَعْلَمُ مَا يَقُولُ حَقًّا لَتَبِعْنَاهُ ‏
en: ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad narrated to us — ʿAbd Allāh said: and I also heard it from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah — Marwān ibn Muʿāwiyah al-Fazārī narrated to us, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Ṭāʾifī, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Khālid al-ʿAdwānī, from his father, that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at the eastern side of Thaqīf. He was standing leaning on a bow or a staff when he came to them seeking their support. He said: I heard him reciting {By the sky and the night-comer} [86:1] until he finished it. He said: I memorized it in the pre-Islamic period while I was still a polytheist, then I recited it in Islam. He said: Thaqīf summoned me and said: "What did you hear from this man?" So I recited it to them. Those from Quraysh who were with them said: "We know our fellow better. If we knew what he says is true, we would have followed him."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نُمَيْرِ بْنِ عَرِيبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَامِرِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ الْجُمَحِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الصَّوْمُ فِي الشِّتَاءِ الْغَنِيمَةُ الْبَارِدَةُ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Abū Isḥāq, from Numayr ibn ʿArīb, from ʿĀmir ibn Masʿūd al-Jumaḥī, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Fasting in winter is the easy spoil."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ كَيْسَانَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَاهُ ‏ ‏أخْبَرَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَتَّجِرُ بِالْخَمْرِ فِي زَمَنِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنَّهُ أَقْبَلَ مِنْ ‏ ‏الشَّامِ ‏ ‏وَمَعَهُ خَمْرٌ فِي ‏ ‏الزِّقَاقِ ‏ ‏يُرِيدُ بِهَا التِّجَارَةَ فَأَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي جِئْتُكَ بِشَرَابٍ جَيِّدٍ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏كَيْسَانُ ‏ ‏إِنَّهَا قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ بَعْدَكَ قَالَ أَفَأَبِيعُهَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّهَا ‏ ‏قَدْ حُرِّمَتْ وَحُرِّمَ ثَمَنُهَا فَانْطَلَقَ ‏ ‏كَيْسَانُ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏الزِّقَاقِ ‏ ‏فَأَخَذَ بِأَرْجُلِهَا ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَهْرَقَهَا ‏
en: Qutaybah narrated to us, Ibn Lahīʿah narrated to us, from Sulaymān ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, from Nāfiʿ ibn Kaysān, that his father informed him that he used to trade in wine during the time of the Prophet ﷺ. He came from Syria with wine in waterskins intending trade. He went to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I have brought you a fine drink." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Kaysān, it has been prohibited after you left." He said: "May I sell it, O Messenger of Allah?" The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "It has been prohibited, and its price has been prohibited." So Kaysān went to the waterskins, took them by their legs, and poured them out.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زُهْرَةَ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ آخِذٌ بِيَدِ ‏ ‏عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَنْهُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ وَاللَّهِ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَأَنْتَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا نَفْسِي فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ ‏ ‏لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى أَكُونَ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ قَالَ فَأَنْتَ الْآنَ وَاللَّهِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ نَفْسِي فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْآنَ يَا ‏ ‏عُمَرُ ‏
en: Qutaybah ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, Ibn Lahīʿah narrated to us, from Zuhrah ibn Maʿbad, from his grandfather, who said: We were with the Prophet ﷺ and he was holding the hand of ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb رضي الله تعالى عنه. ʿUmar said: "By Allah, O Messenger of Allah, you are more beloved to me than everything except myself." The Prophet ﷺ said: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, none of you truly believes until I am more beloved to him than his own self." ʿUmar said: "Then now, by Allah, you are more beloved to me than my own self." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Now, O ʿUmar."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَعْنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَعْنِ بْنِ نَضْلَةَ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الْغِفَارِيُّ مَدِينِيٌّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏جَدِّي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَعْنٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ مَعْنِ بْنِ نَضْلَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَضْلَةَ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الْغِفَارِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏لَقِيَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِمَرَّبَيْنَ ‏ ‏فَهَجَمَ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏شَوَائِلُ ‏ ‏لَهُ فَسَقَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ شَرِبَ فَضْلَةَ إِنَاءٍ فَامْتَلَأَ بِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنْ كُنْتُ لَأَشْرَبُ السَّبْعَةَ فَمَا أَمْتَلِئُ قَالَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ يَشْرَبُ فِي مِعًى وَاحِدٍ وَإِنَّ الْكَافِرَ يَشْرَبُ فِي سَبْعَةِ أَمْعَاءٍ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbd Allāh narrated to us, he said: Muḥammad ibn Maʿn ibn Muḥammad ibn Maʿn ibn Naḍlah ibn ʿAmr al-Ghifārī — a man of Madinah — narrated to me, he said: My grandfather Muḥammad ibn Maʿn narrated to me, from his father Maʿn ibn Naḍlah, from Naḍlah ibn ʿAmr al-Ghifārī, that he met the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at Marrabbayn. His she-camels that had recently given birth rushed at him. He gave the Messenger of Allah ﷺ water to drink, then drank the remainder of a vessel and was filled by it. Then he said: "O Messenger of Allah ﷺ, I used to drink seven vessels and not be full." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The believer drinks in one intestine, and the disbeliever drinks in seven intestines."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَابِرُ بْنُ صُبْحٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏الْمُثَنَّى بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْخُزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏وَصَحِبْتُهُ إِلَى ‏ ‏وَاسِطٍ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ يُسَمِّي فِي أَوَّلِ طَعَامِهِ وَفِي آخِرِ لُقْمَةٍ يَقُولُ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فِي أَوَّلِهِ وَآخِرِهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّكَ تُسَمِّي فِي أَوَّلِ مَا تَأْكُلُ أَرَأَيْتَ قَوْلَكَ فِي آخِرِ مَا تَأْكُلُ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ قَالَ أُخْبِرُكَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ إِنَّ جَدِّي ‏ ‏أُمَيَّةَ بْنَ مَخْشِيٍّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ رَجُلًا كَانَ يَأْكُلُ وَالنَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَنْظُرُ فَلَمْ يُسَمِّ حَتَّى كَانَ فِي آخِرِ طَعَامِهِ لُقْمَةٌ فَقَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا زَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ يَأْكُلُ مَعَهُ حَتَّى سَمَّى فَلَمْ يَبْقَ فِي بَطْنِهِ شَيْءٌ إِلَّا قَاءَهُ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbd Allāh narrated to us, Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, Jābir ibn Ṣubḥ narrated to us, he said: al-Muthannā ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Khuzāʿī narrated to me — and I traveled with him to Wāsiṭ. He used to say the basmalah at the start of his food, and with his last morsel he would say: "In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end." I said to him: "You say the basmalah at the start of your eating. What about your saying at the end of your eating: 'In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end'?" He said: "Let me tell you about that. My grandfather Umayyah ibn Makhshī, who was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ, I heard him say: A man was eating while the Prophet ﷺ watched. He did not say the basmalah until his last morsel remained, then he said: 'In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The devil was eating with him until he said the basmalah. Then nothing remained in his stomach except that he vomited it.'"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحَكَمِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَبِيعَةَ السُّلَمِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي سَفَرٍ فَسَمِعَ مُؤَذِّنًا يَقُولُ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ قَالَ أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدًا ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَشْهَدُ أَنِّي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٌ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تَجِدُونَهُ رَاعِيَ غَنَمٍ أَوْ عَازِبًا عَنْ أَهْلِهِ فَلَمَّا هَبَطَ ‏ ‏الْوَادِي ‏ ‏قَالَ مَرَّ عَلَى ‏ ‏سَخْلَةٍ ‏ ‏مَنْبُوذَةٍ فَقَالَ أَتَرَوْنَ هَذِهِ هَيِّنَةً عَلَى أَهْلِهَا لَلدُّنْيَا أَهْوَنُ عَلَى اللَّهِ مِنْ هَذِهِ عَلَى أَهْلِهَا ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, he said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, from al-Ḥakam, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Rabīʿah al-Sulamī, who said: The Prophet ﷺ was on a journey and heard a muezzin saying: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah." The Prophet ﷺ said: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah." He said: "I bear witness that Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allah." The Prophet ﷺ said: "I bear witness that I am Muḥammad, the Messenger of Allah." Then the Prophet ﷺ said: "You will find him to be a shepherd or one who lives apart from his family." When they descended into the valley, they passed by a discarded dead lamb. He said: "Do you think this is insignificant to its owner? The world is more insignificant to Allah than this is to its owner."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بِشْرُ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو عَبْد الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَحَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو خَيْثَمَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بِشْرُ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَارِثَةَ بْنِ مُضَرِّبٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏فُرَاتِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ بِقَتْلِهِ وَكَانَ عَيْنًا ‏ ‏لِأَبِي سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏وَحَلِيفًا فَمَرَّ بِحَلْقَةٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنِّي مُسْلِمٌ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّهُ يَزْعُمُ أَنَّهُ مُسْلِمٌ فَقَالَ إِنَّ مِنْكُمْ رِجَالًا ‏ ‏نَكِلُهُمْ ‏ ‏إِلَى إِيمَانِهِمْ مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏فُرَاتُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn ʿAbd Allāh narrated to us, Bishr ibn al-Sarrī narrated to us — Abū ʿAbd al-Raḥmān said: and Abū Khaythamah narrated to me, Bishr ibn al-Sarrī narrated to us — Sufyān narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from Ḥārithah ibn Muḍarrib, from Furāt ibn Ḥayyān, that the Prophet ﷺ ordered his execution. He was a spy for Abū Sufyān and an ally. He passed by a circle of the Anṣār and said: "I am a Muslim." They said: "O Messenger of Allah, he claims he is a Muslim." He said: "Among you are men whom we entrust to their faith; among them is Furāt ibn Ḥayyān."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ بَحْرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُغِيرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنِ زِيَادِ بْنِ حِذْيَمٍ السَّعْدِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏حِذْيَمٍ السَّعْدِيِّ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏شَهِدَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏أَلَا إِنَّ دِمَاءَكُمْ وَأَمْوَالَكُمْ وَأَعْرَاضَكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ حَرَامٌ كَحُرْمَةِ يَوْمِكُمْ هَذَا وَكَحُرْمَةِ شَهْرِكُمْ هَذَا وَكَحُرْمَةِ بَلَدِكُمْ هَذَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو عَبْد الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏و حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو خَيْثَمَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مِثْلَهُ ‏
en: ʿAlī ibn Baḥr narrated to us, Jarīr ibn ʿAbd al-Ḥamīd narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Mūsā ibn Ziyād ibn Ḥidhyam al-Saʿdī, from his father, from his grandfather Ḥidhyam al-Saʿdī, that he witnessed the Messenger of Allah ﷺ during the Farewell Pilgrimage. He said: "Indeed, your blood, your wealth, and your honor are sacred to you, like the sanctity of this day of yours, this month of yours, and this land of yours." Abū ʿAbd al-Raḥmān said: and Abū Khaythamah narrated to me, Jarīr narrated to us — and he mentioned the same.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَسْوَدُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَقِيلٍ ‏ ‏قَاضِي ‏ ‏وَاسِطٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَابِقِ بْنِ نَاجِيَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَّامٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ رَجُلٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏حِمْصَ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا هَذَا ‏ ‏خَادِمُ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَقُمْتُ إِلَيْهِ فَقُلْتُ حَدِّثْنِي حَدِيثًا سَمِعْتَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَتَدَاوَلُهُ بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ الرِّجَالُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ مُسْلِمٍ يَقُولُ حِينَ يُصْبِحُ وَحِينَ يُمْسِي ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا ‏ ‏وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَبِيًّا إِلَّا كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يُرْضِيَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: Aswad ibn ʿĀmir narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Abū ʿAqīl — the judge of Wāsiṭ — from Sābiq ibn Nājiyah, from Abū Sallām, who said: A man passed through the mosque of Ḥimṣ, and they said: "This one served the Prophet ﷺ." He said: I went up to him and said: "Tell me a hadith you heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that has not been passed between you and him through other men." He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "No Muslim servant says in the morning and in the evening three times: 'I am pleased with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muḥammad ﷺ as Prophet,' except that it is a right upon Allah to please him on the Day of Resurrection."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مِسْعَرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَقِيلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَابِقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَّامٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَادِمِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَالَ رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا ‏ ‏وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏نَبِيًّا حِينَ يُمْسِي ثَلَاثًا وَحِينَ يُصْبِحُ ثَلَاثًا كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يُرْضِيَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Misʿar narrated to us, from Abū ʿAqīl, from Sābiq, from Abū Sallām, from a servant of the Prophet ﷺ, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: "Whoever says: 'I am pleased with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muḥammad as Prophet' in the evening three times and in the morning three times, it is a right upon Allah to please him on the Day of Resurrection."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي عَقِيلٍ هَاشِمِ بْنِ بِلَالٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَابِقِ بْنِ نَاجِيَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي سَلَّامٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏الْحَبَشِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِهِ رَجُلٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏حِمْصَ ‏ ‏فَقِيلَ هَذَا ‏ ‏خَدَمَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ حَدِّثْنِي حَدِيثًا سَمِعْتَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَمْ يَتَدَاوَلْهُ بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ الرِّجَالُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يَقُولُ حِينَ يُمْسِي وَحِينَ يُصْبِحُ رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا ‏ ‏وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نَبِيًّا ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ إِلَّا كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يُرْضِيَهُ ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Abū ʿAqīl Hāshim ibn Bilāl, from Sābiq ibn Nājiyah, from Abū Sallām — Abū al-Naḍr said: al-Ḥabashī — who said: A man passed by him in the mosque of Ḥimṣ, and it was said: "This one served the Prophet ﷺ." So he went to him and said: "Tell me a hadith you heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that has not been passed between you and him through other men." He said: I heard the Prophet ﷺ saying: "No servant says in the evening and in the morning: 'I am pleased with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muḥammad ﷺ as Prophet' three times, except that it is a right upon Allah to please him."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ غَيْلَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏رِشْدِينُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَكْرُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ هُبَيْرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ حَدَّثَهُ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ خَدَمَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثَمَانِ سِنِينَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا قُرِّبَ لَهُ طَعَامٌ قَالَ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ فَإِذَا فَرَغَ مِنْ طَعَامِهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ أَطْعَمْتَ وَأَسْقَيْتَ وَأَغْنَيْتَ وَأَقْنَيْتَ وَهَدَيْتَ ‏ ‏وَاجْتَبَيْتَ ‏ ‏فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ عَلَى مَا أَعْطَيْتَ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Ghaylān narrated to us, Rishdīn ibn Saʿd narrated to us, Bakr ibn ʿAmr narrated to us, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Hubayrah, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Jubayr, that a man who served the Prophet ﷺ for eight years narrated to him. He said: When food was brought to the Prophet ﷺ, he would say: "In the name of Allah." When he finished eating, he would say: "O Allah, You have fed, given to drink, enriched, made content, guided, and chosen. All praise is due to You for what You have given."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْأَدْرَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ أَحْرُسُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ فَخَرَجَ لِبَعْضِ حَاجَتِهِ قَالَ فَرَآنِي فَأَخَذَ بِيَدِي فَانْطَلَقْنَا فَمَرَرْنَا عَلَى رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي يَجْهَرُ بِالْقُرْآنِ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَسَى أَنْ يَكُونَ ‏ ‏مُرَائِيًا ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي يَجْهَرُ بِالْقُرْآنِ قَالَ فَرَفَضَ يَدِي ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ لَنْ تَنَالُوا هَذَا الْأَمْرَ ‏ ‏بِالْمُغَالَبَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ وَأَنَا أَحْرُسُهُ لِبَعْضِ حَاجَتِهِ فَأَخَذَ بِيَدِي فَمَرَرْنَا عَلَى رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي بِالْقُرْآنِ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ عَسَى أَنْ يَكُونَ ‏ ‏مُرَائِيًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَلَّا إِنَّهُ ‏ ‏أَوَّابٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَنَظَرْتُ فَإِذَا هُوَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ ذُو الْبِجَادَيْنِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Hishām ibn Saʿd informed us, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ibn al-Adraʿ, who said: I was guarding the Prophet ﷺ one night. He went out for some need and saw me. He took my hand and we walked along. We passed by a man who was praying aloud with the Quran. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Perhaps he is showing off." I said: "O Messenger of Allah, he is praying aloud with the Quran." He let go of my hand and said: "You will not attain this matter through overpowering." Then another night, while I was guarding him, he went out for some need. He took my hand and we passed by a man praying with the Quran. I said: "Perhaps he is showing off." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Not at all — he is one who constantly turns to Allah in repentance (awwāb)." I looked, and it was ʿAbd Allāh Dhū al-Bijādayn.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏الْمَسْعُودِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏تُقَاتِلُونَ ‏ ‏جَزِيرَةَ الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهَا اللَّهُ وَتُقَاتِلُونَ ‏ ‏فَارِسَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهُمْ اللَّهُ وَتُقَاتِلُونَ ‏ ‏الرُّومَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهُمْ اللَّهُ وَتُقَاتِلُونَ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهُ اللَّهُ ‏
en: Yazīd narrated to us, al-Masʿūdī informed us, from ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿUmayr, from Jābir ibn Samurah, from Nāfiʿ ibn ʿUtbah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "You will fight the Arabian Peninsula, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight Persia, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight the Romans, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight the Dajjāl, and Allah will open it (grant you victory over him)."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ يَعْنِي الْفَزَارِيَّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نَافِعِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي غَزَاةٍ فَأَتَاهُ قَوْمٌ مِنْ قِبَلِ الْمَغْرِبِ عَلَيْهِمْ ثِيَابُ ‏ ‏الصُّوفِ ‏ ‏فَوَافَقُوهُ عِنْدَ ‏ ‏أَكَمَةٍ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ قِيَامٌ وَهُوَ قَاعِدٌ فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَقُمْتُ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَهُ فَحَفِظْتُ مِنْهُ أَرْبَعَ كَلِمَاتٍ أَعُدُّهُنَّ فِي يَدِي قَالَ ‏ ‏تَغْزُونَ ‏ ‏جَزِيرَةَ الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ تَغْزُونَ ‏ ‏فَارِسَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ تَغْزُونَ ‏ ‏الرُّومَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ تَغْزُونَ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالَ ‏ ‏فَيَفْتَحُهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏نَافِعٌ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏جَابِرُ ‏ ‏أَلَا ‏ ‏تَرَى أَنَّ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالَ ‏ ‏لَا يَخْرُجُ حَتَّى تُفْتَحَ ‏ ‏الرُّومُ ‏
en: Muʿāwiyah ibn ʿAmr narrated to us, Abū Isḥāq — meaning al-Fazārī — narrated to us, from ʿAbd al-Malik ibn ʿUmayr, from Jābir ibn Samurah, from Nāfiʿ ibn ʿUtbah, who said: I was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on an expedition. People came from the west wearing woolen clothes. They met him at a hillock while they were standing and he was sitting. I came and stood between them and him, and I memorized four statements from him, counting them on my hand. He said: "You will fight the Arabian Peninsula, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight Persia, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight the Romans, and Allah will open it. Then you will fight the Dajjāl, and Allah will open it." Nāfiʿ said: "O Jābir, do you not see that the Dajjāl will not emerge until the Romans are conquered?"

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حُسَيْنٌ يَعْنِي الْمُعَلِّمَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ بُرَيْدَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏حَنْظَلَةُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنَ بْنَ الْأَدْرَعِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ فَإِذَا هُوَ بِرَجُلٍ قَدْ قَضَى صَلَاتَهُ وَهُوَ يَتَشَهَّدُ وَهُوَ يَقُولُ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ بِاللَّهِ الْوَاحِدِ الْأَحَدِ ‏ ‏الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏الَّذِي لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ أَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي ذُنُوبِي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ قَالَ فَقَالَ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ غُفِرَ لَهُ قَدْ غُفِرَ لَهُ قَدْ غُفِرَ لَهُ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Ṣamad narrated to us, my father narrated to me, Ḥusayn — meaning al-Muʿallim — narrated to us, from Ibn Buraydah, Ḥanẓalah ibn ʿAlī narrated to me, that Miḥjan ibn al-Adraʿ narrated to him that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered the mosque and found a man who had finished his prayer. He was making the tashahhud and saying: "O Allah, I ask You by Allah, the One, the Only, the Eternal Refuge, Who begets not and was not begotten, and there is none comparable to Him — that You forgive me my sins, for You are the Most Forgiving, the Most Merciful." The Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: "He has been forgiven, he has been forgiven, he has been forgiven" — three times.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَعِيدٍ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنِ بْنِ الْأَدْرَعِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏خَطَبَ النَّاسَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ وَمَا يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ وَمَا يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ وَمَا يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ ثَلَاثًا فَقِيلَ لَهُ وَمَا يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ قَالَ يَجِيءُ ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالُ ‏ ‏فَيَصْعَدُ ‏ ‏أُحُدًا ‏ ‏فَيَنْظُرُ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏فَيَقُولُ لِأَصْحَابِهِ أَتَرَوْنَ هَذَا الْقَصْرَ الْأَبْيَضَ هَذَا ‏ ‏مَسْجِدُ أَحْمَدَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ يَأْتِي ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏فَيَجِدُ بِكُلِّ ‏ ‏نَقْبٍ ‏ ‏مِنْهَا مَلَكًا مُصْلِتًا فَيَأْتِي ‏ ‏سَبْخَةَ ‏ ‏الْحَرْفِ ‏ ‏فَيَضْرِبُ ‏ ‏رُوَاقَهُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ تَرْجُفُ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةُ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثَ رَجَفَاتٍ فَلَا ‏ ‏يَبْقَى مُنَافِقٌ وَلَا مُنَافِقَةٌ وَلَا فَاسِقٌ وَلَا فَاسِقَةٌ إِلَّا خَرَجَ إِلَيْهِ فَذَلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخَلَاصِ ‏
en: Yūnus narrated to us, Ḥammād — meaning ibn Salamah — narrated to us, from Saʿīd al-Jurayrī, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Shaqīq, from Miḥjan ibn al-Adraʿ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed the people and said: "The Day of Salvation — what is the Day of Salvation? The Day of Salvation — what is the Day of Salvation?" — three times. It was said to him: "What is the Day of Salvation?" He said: "The Dajjāl will come and ascend Mount Uḥud. He will look at Madinah and say to his companions: 'Do you see that white palace? That is the Mosque of Aḥmad.' Then he will approach Madinah and find at every pass an angel with a drawn sword. He will come to the salt flat of al-Ḥarf and pitch his tent. Then Madinah will shake with three tremors, and no hypocrite — man or woman — nor any sinner — man or woman — will remain except that they will go out to him. That is the Day of Salvation."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏بُرَيْدَةُ ‏ ‏عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ فَمَرَّ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنٌ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏وَسُكْبَةُ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ ‏ ‏بُرَيْدَةُ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ فِيهِ ‏ ‏مُرَاحٌ ‏ ‏لِمِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏أَلَا تُصَلِّي كَمَا ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي هَذَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنٌ ‏ ‏إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَخَذَ بِيَدِي فَصَعِدَ عَلَى ‏ ‏أُحُدٍ ‏ ‏فَأَشْرَفَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏وَيْلُ أُمِّهَا قَرْيَةً يَدَعُهَا أَهْلُهَا خَيْرَ مَا تَكُونُ ‏ ‏أَوْ كَأَخْيَرِ مَا تَكُونُ ‏ ‏فَيَأْتِيهَا ‏ ‏الدَّجَّالُ ‏ ‏فَيَجِدُ عَلَى كُلِّ بَابٍ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا مَلَكًا مُصْلِتًا جَنَاحَيْهِ فَلَا يَدْخُلُهَا قَالَ ثُمَّ نَزَلَ وَهُوَ آخِذٌ بِيَدِي فَدَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ وَإِذَا هُوَ بِرَجُلٍ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ لِي مَنْ هَذَا فَأَتَيْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَأَثْنَيْتُ عَلَيْهِ خَيْرًا فَقَالَ اسْكُتْ لَا تُسْمِعْهُ فَتُهْلِكَهُ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَى حُجْرَةَ امْرَأَةٍ مِنْ نِسَائِهِ فَنَفَضَ يَدَهُ مِنْ يَدِي قَالَ إِنَّ خَيْرَ دِينِكُمْ أَيْسَرُهُ إِنَّ خَيْرَ دِينِكُمْ أَيْسُرُهُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي بِشْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ الْبَاهِلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ مَعْنَاهُ وَلَمْ يَقُلْ ‏ ‏حَجَّاجٌ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏بِجَنَاحِهِ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Abū Bishr, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Shaqīq, from Rajāʾ ibn Abī Rajāʾ, who said: Buraydah was at the door of the mosque. Miḥjan passed by him while Sukbah was praying. Buraydah — who was inclined to jest with Miḥjan — said: "Why do you not pray as this one prays?" Miḥjan said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ took my hand and climbed Mount Uḥud. He overlooked Madinah and said: 'Woe to its mother — a town whose people will abandon it when it is at its best, or nearly at its best. Then the Dajjāl will come and find at every gate of its gates an angel with a drawn sword, so he will not enter it.' Then he descended, still holding my hand, and entered the mosque. There was a man praying. He said to me: 'Who is this?' I went over to him and praised him highly. He said: 'Be silent — do not let him hear, lest you ruin him.' Then he went to the chamber of one of his wives and released my hand. He said: 'The best of your religion is its easiest. The best of your religion is its easiest.'" Ḥajjāj narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Abū Bishr, who said: I heard ʿAbd Allāh ibn Shaqīq narrating from Rajāʾ ibn Abī Rajāʾ al-Bāhilī, from Miḥjan — a man from Aslam — and he mentioned its meaning, except Ḥajjāj and Abū al-Naḍr did not say: "with his wing (sword)."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏مَرَّةً ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُسْرٍ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏بُسْرِ بْنِ مِحْجَنٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ كَانَ يَقُولُ بَعْدُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي مِحْجَنٍ الدِّيلِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَحَضَرَتْ الصَّلَاةُ فَصَلَّى فَقَالَ لِي أَلَا صَلَّيْتَ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَدْ صَلَّيْتُ فِي الرَّحْلِ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُكَ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا فَعَلْتَ فَصَلِّ مَعَهُمْ وَاجْعَلْهَا ‏ ‏نَافِلَةً ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏وَلَمْ يَقُلْ ‏ ‏أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ ‏ ‏وَلَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَاجْعَلْهَا ‏ ‏نَافِلَةً ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Zayd ibn Aslam — Sufyān said once: from Busr, or Busr ibn Miḥjan; then he later said: from Abū Miḥjan al-Dīlī — from his father, who said: I came to the Prophet ﷺ while he was in the mosque. Prayer time arrived, and he prayed. He said to me: "Did you not pray?" I said: "O Messenger of Allah, I had already prayed at my camp, then I came to you." He said: "If you have done so, then pray with them and make it a voluntary prayer." His father said: Abū Nuʿaym and ʿAbd al-Raḥmān did not include: "and make it a voluntary prayer."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُرَيْجُ بْنُ النُّعْمَانِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَقِيَّةُ بْنُ الْوَلِيدِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ سُلَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنِ جَابِرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ضَمْرَةَ بْنِ ثَعْلَبَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَعَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏حُلَّتَانِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏حُلَلِ ‏ ‏الْيَمَنِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏ضَمْرَةُ ‏ ‏أَتَرَى ثَوْبَيْكَ هَذَيْنِ مُدْخِلَيْكَ الْجَنَّةَ فَقَالَ لَئِنْ اسْتَغْفَرْتَ لِي يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَا أَقْعُدُ حَتَّى أَنْزَعَهُمَا عَنِّي فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ ‏ ‏لِضَمْرَةَ بْنِ ثَعْلَبَةَ ‏ ‏فَانْطَلَقَ سَرِيعًا حَتَّى نَزَعَهُمَا عَنْهُ ‏
en: Surayj ibn al-Nuʿmān narrated to us, Baqiyyah ibn al-Walīd narrated to us, from Sulaymān ibn Sulaym, from Yaḥyā ibn Jābir, from Ḍamrah ibn Thaʿlabah, that he came to the Prophet ﷺ wearing two garments from the garments of Yemen. The Prophet ﷺ said: "O Ḍamrah, do you think these two garments of yours will admit you to Paradise?" He said: "If you seek forgiveness for me, O Messenger of Allah, I will not sit still until I remove them." The Prophet ﷺ said: "O Allah, forgive Ḍamrah ibn Thaʿlabah." He hurried away until he removed them.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ بَحِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ضِرَارِ بْنِ الْأَزْوَرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَنِي أَهْلِي ‏ ‏بِلَقُوحٍ ‏ ‏إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أَحْلُبَهَا فَحَلَبْتُهَا فَقَالَ لِي ‏ ‏دَعْ ‏ ‏دَاعِيَ ‏ ‏اللَّبَنِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, al-Aʿmash narrated to us, from Yaʿqūb ibn Baḥīr, from Ḍirār ibn al-Azwar, who said: My family sent me with a milch-camel to the Prophet ﷺ. He ordered me to milk it, so I milked it. He said to me: "Leave the caller of the milk" — meaning leave some milk in the udder to encourage continued production.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَسْوَدُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زُهَيْرٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ بَحِيرٍ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ مِنْ الْحَيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏ضِرَارَ بْنَ الْأَزْوَرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَهْدَيْنَا لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِقْحَةً ‏ ‏قَالَ فَحَلَبْتُهَا قَالَ فَلَمَّا أَخَذْتُ ‏ ‏لِأُجْهِدَهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَا تَفْعَلْ دَعْ دَاعِيَ اللَّبَنِ ‏
en: Aswad ibn ʿĀmir narrated to us, Zuhayr narrated to us, from al-Aʿmash, from Yaʿqūb ibn Baḥīr — a man from the tribe — who said: I heard Ḍirār ibn al-Azwar say: We gifted the Messenger of Allah ﷺ a milch-camel. I milked it, and when I began to milk it dry, he said: "Do not do that. Leave the caller of the milk."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ضِرَارِ بْنِ الْأَزْوَرِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَرَّ بِهِ وَهُوَ يَحْلُبُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏دَعْ ‏ ‏دَاعِيَ ‏ ‏اللَّبَنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْد اللَّهِ ‏ ‏و حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكَّارٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏أَوْ عَنْ ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ بَحِيرٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ضِرَارِ بْنِ الْأَزْوَرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِنَحْوِهِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from al-Aʿmash, from ʿAbd Allāh ibn Sinān, from Ḍirār ibn al-Azwar, that the Prophet ﷺ passed by him while he was milking and said: "Leave the caller of the milk." ʿAbd Allāh said: and Muḥammad ibn Bakkār narrated to me, ʿAbd Allāh ibn al-Mubārak narrated to us, al-Aʿmash narrated to us — or from al-Aʿmash — from Yaʿqūb ibn Baḥīr, from Ḍirār ibn al-Azwar, from the Prophet ﷺ — a similar narration.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو إِسْرَائِيلَ الْجُشَمِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَيْخٍ لَهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏جَعْدَةُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَأَى لِرَجُلٍ رُؤْيَا قَالَ فَبَعَثَ إِلَيْهِ فَجَاءَ فَجَعَلَ يَقُصُّهَا عَلَيْهِ وَكَانَ الرَّجُلُ عَظِيمَ الْبَطْنِ قَالَ فَجَعَلَ يَقُولُ بِأُصْبُعِهِ فِي بَطْنِهِ لَوْ كَانَ هَذَا فِي غَيْرِ هَذَا لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَكَ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, Abū Isrāʾīl al-Jushamī narrated to us, from an elder of theirs called Jaʿdah, that the Prophet ﷺ had a dream about a man. He sent for him, and the man came and began recounting it to him. The man had a large belly. The Prophet kept pointing at his belly with his finger, saying: "If this were in something other than this, it would be better for you."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏السَّائِبِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَمْكُثُ الْمُهَاجِرُ ‏ ‏بِمَكَّةَ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ قَضَاءِ ‏ ‏نُسُكِهِ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا كَانَ أَشَدَّ عَلَى ‏ ‏ابْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَقُولَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏
en: Sufyān ibn ʿUyaynah narrated to us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Ḥumayd ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿAwf narrated to me, from al-Sāʾib ibn Yazīd, from al-ʿAlāʾ ibn al-Ḥaḍramī — if Allah wills — that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The emigrant may stay in Makkah after the completion of his pilgrimage rites for three days." He said: It was very hard for Ibn ʿUyaynah to say "narrated to us."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٌ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ سِيرِينَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا بِهِ ‏ ‏هُشَيْمٌ ‏ ‏مَرَّتَيْنِ مَرَّةً عَنْ ‏ ‏ابْنِ الْعَلَاءِ ‏ ‏وَمَرَّةً لَمْ يَصِلْ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَاهُ ‏ ‏كَتَبَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَبَدَأَ بِنَفْسِهِ ‏
en: Hushaym narrated to us, Manṣūr narrated to us, from Ibn Sīrīn, from the son of al-ʿAlāʾ ibn al-Ḥaḍramī — his father said: Hushaym narrated it to us twice: once from Ibn al-ʿAlāʾ, and once without connecting the chain — that his father wrote to the Prophet ﷺ and began with himself (putting his own name before the Prophet's name).

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ ‏ ‏فَانْتَثِرْ ‏ ‏وَإِذَا ‏ ‏اسْتَجْمَرْتَ ‏ ‏فَأَوْتِرْ ‏
en: Sufyān ibn ʿUyaynah narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Hilāl ibn Yasāf, from Salamah ibn Qays, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When you make ablution, blow water from your nose, and when you use stones for cleaning, use an odd number."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ ‏ ‏فَانْتَثِرْ ‏ ‏وَإِذَا ‏ ‏اسْتَجْمَرْتَ ‏ ‏فَأَوْتِرْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Manṣūr, from Hilāl ibn Yasāf, from Salamah ibn Qays, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When you make ablution, blow water from your nose, and when you use stones for cleaning, use an odd number."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ ‏ ‏إِنَّمَا هُنَّ أَرْبَعٌ لَا تُشْرِكُوا بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تَسْرِقُوا وَلَا تَزْنُوا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Manṣūr, from Hilāl ibn Yasāf, from Salamah ibn Qays, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: "They are only four: Do not associate anything with Allah, do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, do not steal, and do not commit adultery."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ يَعْنِي شَيْبَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ الْأَشْجَعِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ ‏ ‏أَلَا إِنَّمَا هُنَّ أَرْبَعٌ أَنْ لَا تُشْرِكُوا بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تَزْنُوا وَلَا تَسْرِقُوا قَالَ فَمَا أَنَا ‏ ‏بِأَشَحَّ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنِّي إِذْ سَمِعْتُهُنَّ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏
en: Hāshim narrated to us, he said: Abū Muʿāwiyah — meaning Shaybān — narrated to us, Manṣūr narrated to us, from Hilāl ibn Yasāf, from Salamah ibn Qays al-Ashjaʿī, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: "Indeed, they are only four: that you do not associate anything with Allah, that you do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, that you do not commit adultery, and that you do not steal." He said: "I am no more careful about guarding them now than when I heard them from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَعْمَرٌ ‏ ‏وَالثَّوْرِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا تَوَضَّأْتَ ‏ ‏فَانْتَثِرْ ‏ ‏وَإِذَا ‏ ‏اسْتَجْمَرْتَ ‏ ‏فَأَوْتِرْ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, Maʿmar and al-Thawrī narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Hilāl ibn Yasāf, from Salamah ibn Qays, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to me: "When you make ablution, blow water from your nose, and when you use stones for cleaning, use an odd number."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَوْلَى الْقَوْمِ مِنْهُمْ وَابْنُ أُخْتِهِمْ مِنْهُمْ وَحَلِيفُهُمْ مِنْهُمْ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ibn Khuthaym, from Ismāʿīl ibn ʿUbayd ibn Rifāʿah, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The freed slave of a people is from them, the son of their sister is from them, and their ally is from them."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏وَكِيعٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُفْيَانُ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏ابْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَمَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ هَلْ فِيكُمْ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ قَالُوا لَا إِلَّا ابْنُ أُخْتِنَا وَحَلِيفُنَا وَمَوْلَانَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ أُخْتِكُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَحَلِيفُكُمْ مِنْكُمْ وَمَوْلَاكُمْ مِنْكُمْ إِنَّ ‏ ‏قُرَيْشًا ‏ ‏أَهْلُ صِدْقٍ وَأَمَانَةٍ فَمَنْ ‏ ‏بَغَى ‏ ‏لَهَا ‏ ‏الْعَوَائِرَ ‏ ‏أَكَبَّهُ ‏ ‏اللَّهُ فِي النَّارِ لِوَجْهِهِ ‏
en: Wakīʿ narrated to us, Sufyān narrated to us, from Ibn Khuthaym, from Ismāʿīl ibn ʿUbayd ibn Rifāʿah, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ gathered Quraysh and said: "Are there among you any who are not of you?" They said: "No, except our sister's son, our ally, and our freed slave." He said: "The son of your sister is from you, your ally is from you, and your freed slave is from you. Quraysh are people of truthfulness and trustworthiness. Whoever seeks to bring calamity upon them, Allah will cast him into the Fire on his face."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بِشْرٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْمُفَضَّلِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ بْنِ رَافِعٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَدِّهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَلِيفُنَا مِنَّا وَمَوْلَانَا مِنَّا وَابْنُ أُخْتِنَا مِنَّا ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Bishr — meaning ibn al-Mufaḍḍal — narrated to us, ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿUthmān ibn Khuthaym narrated to us, from Ismāʿīl ibn ʿUbayd ibn Rifāʿah ibn Rāfiʿ al-Zuraqī, from his father, from his grandfather, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Our ally is from us, and our sister's son is from us."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ خَلَّادٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رِفَاعَةَ بْنِ رَافِعٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏جَالِسٌ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَصَلَّى قَرِيبًا مِنْهُ ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَعِدْ صَلَاتَكَ فَإِنَّكَ لَمْ تُصَلِّ قَالَ فَرَجَعَ فَصَلَّى كَنَحْوٍ مِمَّا صَلَّى ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ أَعِدْ صَلَاتَكَ فَإِنَّكَ لَمْ تُصَلِّ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّمْنِي كَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِذَا اسْتَقْبَلْتَ الْقِبْلَةَ فَكَبِّرْ ثُمَّ اقْرَأْ ‏ ‏بِأُمِّ الْقُرْآنِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ اقْرَأْ بِمَا شِئْتَ فَإِذَا رَكَعْتَ فَاجْعَلْ ‏ ‏رَاحَتَيْكَ ‏ ‏عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْكَ وَامْدُدْ ظَهْرَكَ وَمَكِّنْ لِرُكُوعِكَ فَإِذَا رَفَعْتَ رَأْسَكَ فَأَقِمْ صُلْبَكَ حَتَّى تَرْجِعَ الْعِظَامُ إِلَى مَفَاصِلِهَا وَإِذَا سَجَدْتَ فَمَكِّنْ لِسُجُودِكَ فَإِذَا رَفَعْتَ رَأْسَكَ فَاجْلِسْ عَلَى فَخِذِكَ الْيُسْرَى ثُمَّ اصْنَعْ ذَلِكَ فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ وَسَجْدَةٍ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Muḥammad ibn ʿAmr informed us, from ʿAlī ibn Yaḥyā ibn Khallād al-Zuraqī, from Rifāʿah ibn Rāfiʿ al-Zuraqī — and he was a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ — who said: A man came while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting in the mosque. He prayed near him, then came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Repeat your prayer, for you have not prayed." He went back and prayed in a similar manner, then came back to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said to him: "Repeat your prayer, for you have not prayed." The man said: "O Messenger of Allah, teach me how to do it." He said: "When you face the qiblah, say the takbīr. Then recite the Mother of the Quran (al-Fātiḥah), then recite whatever you wish. When you bow, place your palms on your knees, extend your back, and settle firmly in your bowing. When you raise your head, straighten your spine until the bones return to their joints. When you prostrate, settle firmly in your prostration. When you raise your head, sit on your left thigh. Then do that in every rakʿah and prostration."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَرَأْتُ عَلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏: ‏ ‏مَالِكٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏نُعَيْمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُجْمِرِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلِيِّ بْنِ يَحْيَى الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رِفَاعَةَ بْنِ رَافِعٍ الزُّرَقِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا نُصَلِّي يَوْمًا وَرَاءَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا رَفَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَأْسَهُ مِنْ الرَّكْعَةِ وَقَالَ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏وَرَاءَهُ رَبَّنَا لَكَ الْحَمْدُ حَمْدًا كَثِيرًا طَيِّبًا مُبَارَكًا فِيهِ فَلَمَّا انْصَرَفَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ مَنْ الْمُتَكَلِّمُ ‏ ‏آنِفًا ‏ ‏قَالَ الرَّجُلُ أَنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَقَدْ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ بِضْعَةً وَثَلَاثِينَ مَلَكًا ‏ ‏يَبْتَدِرُونَهَا ‏ ‏أَيُّهُمْ يَكْتُبُهَا أَوَّلًا ‏
en: He said: I read to ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī: Mālik, from Nuʿaym ibn ʿAbd Allāh al-Mujmir, from ʿAlī ibn Yaḥyā al-Zuraqī, from his father, from Rifāʿah ibn Rāfiʿ al-Zuraqī رضي الله عنه who said: We were praying one day behind the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ raised his head from the bowing and said, 'Semiʿa Allāhu liman ḥamidah (Allah hears the one who praises Him),' a man behind him said, 'Rabbanā lakal-ḥamd, ḥamdan kathīran ṭayyiban mubārakan fīh (Our Lord, to You belongs praise, abundant, pure, and blessed praise).' When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ finished, he said, 'Who was the one who spoke just now?' The man said, 'I was, O Messenger of Allah.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'I saw over thirty angels racing to see which of them would record it first.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ عَجْلَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ خَلَّادٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ بَدْرِيًّا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَدَخَلَ رَجُلٌ فَصَلَّى فِي نَاحِيَةِ الْمَسْجِدِ فَجَعَلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَرْمُقُهُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ جَاءَ فَسَلَّمَ فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ وَقَالَ ارْجِعْ فَصَلِّ فَإِنَّكَ لَمْ تُصَلِّ قَالَ مَرَّتَيْنِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ثَلَاثًا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ فِي الثَّالِثَةِ ‏ ‏أَوْ فِي الرَّابِعَةِ ‏ ‏وَالَّذِي بَعَثَكَ بِالْحَقِّ لَقَدْ أَجْهَدْتُ نَفْسِي فَعَلِّمْنِي وَأَرِنِي فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ فَتَوَضَّأْ فَأَحْسِنْ وُضُوءَكَ ثُمَّ اسْتَقْبِلْ الْقِبْلَةَ ثُمَّ كَبِّرْ ثُمَّ اقْرَأْ ثُمَّ ارْكَعْ حَتَّى تَطْمَئِنَّ رَاكِعًا ثُمَّ ارْفَعْ حَتَّى تَطْمَئِنَّ قَائِمًا ثُمَّ اسْجُدْ حَتَّى تَطْمَئِنَّ سَاجِدًا ثُمَّ ارْفَعْ حَتَّى تَطْمَئِنَّ جَالِسًا ثُمَّ اسْجُدْ حَتَّى تَطْمَئِنَّ سَاجِدًا ثُمَّ قُمْ فَإِذَا أَتْمَمْتَ صَلَاتَكَ عَلَى هَذَا فَقَدْ أَتْمَمْتَهَا وَمَا انْتَقَصْتَ مِنْ هَذَا مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَإِنَّمَا تُنْقِصُهُ مِنْ صَلَاتِكَ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, Ibn ʿAjlān narrated to us, ʿAlī ibn Yaḥyā ibn Khallād narrated to us, from his father, from his uncle — who was a veteran of Badr — who said: We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the mosque when a man entered and prayed in a corner of the mosque. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ kept watching him. Then the man came and greeted him, and he returned the greeting and said, 'Go back and pray, for you have not prayed.' He said this two or three times. Then the man said on the third or fourth occasion, 'By the One who sent you with the truth, I have exerted my utmost effort, so teach me and show me.' The Prophet ﷺ said to him, 'When you intend to pray, perform ablution well, then face the qiblah, then say the takbīr, then recite, then bow until you are at ease in your bowing, then rise until you are at ease standing, then prostrate until you are at ease in your prostration, then rise until you are at ease sitting, then prostrate until you are at ease in your prostration, then stand. When you complete your prayer in this manner, you have completed it, and whatever you fall short of in this, you only diminish from your prayer.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏طَارِقُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْقُرَشِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ ‏ ‏رَافِعُ بْنُ رِفَاعَةَ ‏ ‏إِلَى مَجْلِسِ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَقَدْ نَهَانَا نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْيَوْمَ عَنْ شَيْءٍ كَانَ يَرْفُقُ بِنَا فِي مَعَايِشِنَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏نَهَانَا عَنْ ‏ ‏كِرَاءِ ‏ ‏الْأَرْضِ قَالَ مَنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ أَرْضٌ فَلْيَزْرَعْهَا أَوْ لِيُزْرِعْهَا أَخَاهُ أَوْ لِيَدَعْهَا وَنَهَانَا عَنْ كَسْبِ ‏ ‏الْحَجَّامِ ‏ ‏وَأَمَرَنَا أَنْ نُطْعِمَهُ ‏ ‏نَوَاضِحَنَا ‏ ‏وَنَهَانَا عَنْ كَسْبِ الْأَمَةِ إِلَّا مَا عَمِلَتْ بِيَدِهَا وَقَالَ هَكَذَا بِأَصَابِعِهِ نَحْوَ الْخَبْزِ وَالْغَزْلِ وَالنَّفْشِ ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, ʿIkrimah — meaning Ibn ʿAmmār — narrated to us, he said: Ṭāriq ibn ʿAbd al-Raḥmān al-Qurashī narrated to me, he said: Rāfiʿ ibn Rifāʿah came to a gathering of the Anṣār and said, 'The Prophet of Allah ﷺ has forbidden us today from things that were beneficial to us in our livelihoods.' He said, 'He forbade us from renting out land. He said: Whoever has land should cultivate it himself, or let his brother cultivate it, or leave it fallow. And he forbade us from the earnings of a cupper, and he commanded us to feed it to our water-carrying camels. And he forbade us from the earnings of a slave-woman except what she earns with her own hands,' and he gestured like this with his fingers, indicating baking, spinning, and carding.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شَيْبَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِيَادِ بْنِ عِلَاقَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَرْفَجَةَ بْنِ شُرَيْحٍ الْأَسْلَمِيِّ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّهَا سَتَكُونُ بَعْدِي ‏ ‏هَنَاتٌ ‏ ‏وَهَنَاتٌ ‏ ‏وَرَفَعَ يَدَيْهِ فَمَنْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ يُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أُمَّةِ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ جَمِيعٌ فَاقْتُلُوهُ كَائِنًا مَنْ كَانَ مِنْ النَّاسِ ‏
en: Abū al-Naḍr narrated to us, Shaybān narrated to us, from Ziyād ibn ʿIlāqah, from ʿArfajah ibn Shurayḥ al-Aslamī رضي الله عنه who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'There will be after me trials and tribulations' — and he raised his hands — 'so whoever you see seeking to divide the ummah of Muḥammad ﷺ while they are united, kill him, whoever he may be among the people.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِيَادِ بْنِ عِلَاقَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏عَرْفَجَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِنَّهُ سَتَكُونُ ‏ ‏هَنَاتٌ ‏ ‏وَهَنَاتٌ ‏ ‏فَمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُفَرِّقَ أَمْرَ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ وَهِيَ جَمِيعٌ فَاضْرِبُوهُ بِالسَّيْفِ كَائِنًا مَنْ كَانَ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, Shuʿbah narrated to us, from Ziyād ibn ʿIlāqah who said: I heard ʿArfajah رضي الله عنه say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, 'There will be trials and tribulations, so whoever seeks to divide the affair of this ummah while it is united, strike him with the sword, whoever he may be.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَبَّادَ بْنَ تَمِيمٍ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ ‏ ‏عُوَيْمِرِ بْنِ أَشْقَرَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ذَبَحَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْدُوَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَنَّهُ ذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَعْدَمَا فَرَغَ ‏ ‏فَأَمَرَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَعُودَ لِأُضْحِيَّتِهِ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Yaḥyā — meaning Ibn Saʿīd — informed us that ʿAbbād ibn Tamīm informed him, from ʿUwaymir ibn Ashqar رضي الله عنه, that he slaughtered his sacrifice before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out for the Eid prayer, and that he mentioned this to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ afterward. When he finished, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered him to repeat his sacrifice.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ الْخَطْمِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ كَعْبٍ الْقُرَظِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏كَثِيرِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏ابْنَا قُرَيْظَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُمْ ‏ ‏عُرِضُوا عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏زَمَنَ ‏ ‏قُرَيْظَةَ ‏ ‏فَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏مُحْتَلِمًا ‏ ‏أَوْ نَبَتَتْ ‏ ‏عَانَتُهُ ‏ ‏قُتِلَ وَمَنْ لَا تُرِكَ ‏
en: ʿAffān narrated to us, Ḥammād ibn Salamah narrated to us, from Abū Jaʿfar al-Khaṭmī, from Muḥammad ibn Kaʿb al-Quraẓī, from Kathīr ibn al-Sāʾib who said: The sons of Qurayẓah narrated to me that they were presented before the Prophet ﷺ at the time of the Qurayẓah affair. Whoever among them had reached puberty or whose pubic hair had grown was killed, and whoever had not was spared.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏بُشَيْرِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْحُصَيْنِ بْنِ مِحْصَنٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّ عَمَّةً لَهُ أَتَتْ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي حَاجَةٍ فَفَرَغَتْ مِنْ حَاجَتِهَا فَقَالَ لَهَا النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَذَاتُ زَوْجٍ أَنْتِ قَالَتْ نَعَمْ قَالَ كَيْفَ أَنْتِ لَهُ قَالَتْ مَا ‏ ‏آلُوهُ ‏ ‏إِلَّا مَا عَجَزْتُ عَنْهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَانْظُرِي أَيْنَ أَنْتِ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّمَا هُوَ جَنَّتُكِ وَنَارُكِ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, he said: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd informed us, from Bushayr ibn Yasār, from al-Ḥuṣayn ibn Miḥṣan, that an aunt of his came to the Prophet ﷺ for a need. When she had finished her need, the Prophet ﷺ said to her, 'Do you have a husband?' She said, 'Yes.' He said, 'How are you toward him?' She said, 'I spare no effort for him except what I am unable to do.' He said, 'Then look to where you stand with him, for he is your Paradise and your Fire.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏رَجُلٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏رَبِيعَةُ بْنُ عَبَّادٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي الدِّيلِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ جَاهِلِيًّا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فِي سُوقِ ‏ ‏ذِي الْمَجَازِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَقُولُ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قُولُوا ‏ ‏لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ تُفْلِحُوا وَالنَّاسُ مُجْتَمِعُونَ عَلَيْهِ وَوَرَاءَهُ رَجُلٌ ‏ ‏وَضِيءُ ‏ ‏الْوَجْهِ أَحْوَلُ ذُو ‏ ‏غَدِيرَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ إِنَّهُ ‏ ‏صَابِئٌ ‏ ‏كَاذِبٌ يَتْبَعُهُ حَيْثُ ذَهَبَ فَسَأَلْتُ عَنْهُ فَذَكَرُوا لِي نَسَبَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَالُوا لِي هَذَا عَمُّهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو لَهَبٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سُرَيْجٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَبِيعَةَ بْنِ عَبَّادٍ الدُّؤَلِيِّ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ جَاهِلِيًّا فَأَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا قَالَ هَذَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَذْكُرُ النُّبُوَّةَ قُلْتُ مَنْ هَذَا الَّذِي يُكَذِّبُهُ قَالُوا ‏ ‏هَذَا عَمُّهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو لَهَبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبُو الزِّنَادِ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ ‏ ‏لِرَبِيعَةَ بْنِ عَبَّادٍ ‏ ‏إِنَّكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ كُنْتَ صَغِيرًا قَالَ لَا وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي يَوْمَئِذٍ لَأَعْقِلُ أَنِّي ‏ ‏لَأَزْفِرُ ‏ ‏الْقِرْبَةَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي أَحْمِلُهَا ‏
en: Ibrāhīm ibn Abī al-ʿAbbās narrated to us, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī al-Zinād narrated to us, from his father who said: A man called Rabīʿah ibn ʿAbbād from the Banū al-Dīl informed me — and he had lived in the Jāhiliyyah — he said: I saw the Prophet ﷺ in the Jāhiliyyah in the market of Dhū al-Majāz, and he was saying, 'O people, say lā ilāha illa Allāh and you will prosper.' The people were gathered around him, and behind him was a man with a radiant face, cross-eyed, with two braids, who was saying, 'He is an apostate, a liar,' following him wherever he went. I asked about him, and they told me the lineage of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and they said to me, 'This is his uncle Abū Lahab.' Surayj narrated to us, Ibn Abī al-Zinād narrated to us, from his father, from Rabīʿah ibn ʿAbbād al-Duʾalī — who had lived in the Jāhiliyyah and then accepted Islam — who said: I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — and he mentioned the hadith. He said: I asked, 'Who is this?' They said, 'This is Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib,' and he was proclaiming prophethood. I said, 'Who is this one calling him a liar?' They said, 'This is his uncle Abū Lahab.' Abū al-Zinād said: I said to Rabīʿah ibn ʿAbbād, 'You were a small child that day.' He said, 'No, by Allah, that day I was old enough to understand. I was carrying a waterskin on my back.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو الْأَشْهَبِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ طَرَفَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ جَدَّهُ ‏ ‏عَرْفَجَةَ ‏ ‏أُصِيبَ أَنْفُهُ يَوْمَ ‏ ‏الْكُلَابِ ‏ ‏فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَاتَّخَذَ أَنْفًا مِنْ وَرِقٍ فَأَنْتَنَ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏فَأَمَرَهُ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ أَنْفًا مِنْ ذَهَبٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ ‏ ‏فَقِيلَ ‏ ‏لِأَبِي الْأَشْهَبِ ‏ ‏أَدْرَكْتَ ‏ ‏عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏جَدَّهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏نَعَمْ ‏
en: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, Abū al-Ash·hab informed us, from ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Ṭarafah, that his grandfather ʿArfajah رضي الله عنه had his nose struck off on the Day of al-Kulāb during the Jāhiliyyah, so he fashioned a nose of silver, but it developed a foul smell. The Prophet ﷺ then commanded him to fashion a nose of gold. Yazīd said: It was asked of Abū al-Ash·hab, 'Did you meet ʿAbd al-Raḥmān, his grandfather?' He said, 'Yes.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءِ يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَرَامِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏سَأَلَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَمَّا يُوجِبُ الْغُسْلَ وَعَنْ الْمَاءِ يَكُونُ بَعْدَ الْمَاءِ وَعَنْ الصَّلَاةِ فِي بَيْتِي وَعَنْ الصَّلَاةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَعَنْ مُؤَاكَلَةِ الْحَائِضِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا ‏ ‏يَسْتَحْيِي مِنْ الْحَقِّ وَأَمَّا أَنَا فَإِذَا فَعَلْتُ كَذَا وَكَذَا فَذَكَرَ الْغُسْلَ قَالَ أَتَوَضَّأُ وُضُوئِي لِلصَّلَاةِ أَغْسِلُ فَرْجِي ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ الْغُسْلَ وَأَمَّا الْمَاءُ يَكُونُ بَعْدَ ‏ ‏الْمَاءِ ‏ ‏فَذَلِكَ ‏ ‏الْمَذْيُ ‏ ‏وَكُلُّ ‏ ‏فَحْلٍ ‏ ‏يُمْذِي ‏ ‏فَأَغْسِلُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَرْجِي وَأَتَوَضَّأُ وَأَمَّا الصَّلَاةُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَالصَّلَاةُ فِي بَيْتِي فَقَدْ ‏ ‏تَرَى مَا أَقْرَبَ بَيْتِي مِنْ الْمَسْجِدِ وَلَأَنْ أُصَلِّيَ فِي بَيْتِي أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ أُصَلِّيَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ صَلَاةً مَكْتُوبَةً وَأَمَّا مُؤَاكَلَةُ الْحَائِضِ فَآكِلْهَا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Muʿāwiyah — meaning Ibn Ṣāliḥ — from al-ʿAlāʾ — meaning Ibn al-Ḥārith — from Ḥarām ibn Ḥakīm, from his uncle ʿAbd Allāh ibn Saʿd رضي الله عنه, that he asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about what necessitates ghusl, and about the fluid that comes after urine, and about praying in his house versus praying in the mosque, and about eating with a menstruating woman. He ﷺ said, 'Indeed, Allah عز وجل is not shy of the truth. As for me, when I do such-and-such' — and he described the ghusl — he said, 'I perform ablution as I would for prayer, I wash my private parts, then' — he described the ghusl. 'As for the fluid that comes after urine, that is madhī (pre-seminal fluid), and every male produces madhī, so I wash my private parts from it and perform ablution. As for praying in the mosque versus praying in my house, you can see how close my house is to the mosque, yet for me to pray in my house is more beloved to me than to pray in the mosque, except for the obligatory prayer. As for eating with a menstruating woman, then eat with her.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ ‏ ‏عَنِ ‏ ‏الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَرَامِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ مُؤَاكَلَةِ الْحَائِضِ فَقَالَ وَاكِلْهَا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, Muʿāwiyah ibn Ṣāliḥ narrated to us, from al-ʿAlāʾ ibn al-Ḥārith, from Ḥarām ibn Ḥakīm, from his uncle ʿAbd Allāh ibn Saʿd رضي الله عنه who said: I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about eating with a menstruating woman. He said, 'Eat with her.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ لَهِيعَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَكْرُ بْنُ سَوَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ مَوْلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَانَ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏لِجَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ‏ ‏أَشْبَهْتَ خَلْقِي وَخُلُقِي ‏
en: Ḥasan ibn Mūsā narrated to us, Ibn Lahīʿah narrated to us, Bakr ibn Sawādah narrated to us, from ʿUbayd Allāh ibn Aslam, the freed slave of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say to Jaʿfar ibn Abī Ṭālib رضي الله عنه, 'You resemble me in appearance and in character.'
